programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A hospital caretaker is found dead on the beach in what appears to be a routine case with no suspicion of foul play - until his identical twin brother turns up to identify the body. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan tries to help a former student who has been charged with murder, but it is no simple matter as the man has been diagnosed as a schizophrenic. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: While working as a motel handyman, Kimble tutors the girlfriend of a bookie under police surveillance

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A radio audience, including Perry, listens in horror when a shock jock calls an executive only to hear him arguing with his ex-wife, followed by gunshots

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex rekindles her relationship with an old flame, leaving Walker struggling with his feelings

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The death of a seemingly happy and healthy bride moments before her wedding leaves Mac and Danny with a complex puzzle, especially as the doves due to be released after the ceremony have also been killed. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden track down the corpse of a man who apparently chewed off his own hand, and struggle to ascertain the significance of a hole next to the body. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his old flame, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. Travelling to the crime scene to conduct a joint murder-kidnapping investigation, Dinozzi and McGee offer their assistance in searching for the missing doctors. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray, with Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a dishonourably discharged marine is accused of beating a Japanese tourist to death, having already been linked with the rape of a businessman's daughter. He claims that he is in love with the girl he is alleged to have raped, and that the accusations being thrown in his direction are a fabrication initiated by her father

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble when he is tracked down by Gerard

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry faces a strange set of mysteries - a plane crash, a missing drug supply and a man leading a double life - when he defends a woman accused of murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker and Trivete guard a dignitary who embarks on a peace mission to unite the IRA and Great Britain

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a naval seaman and an infamous arms dealer are found dead near the Mexican border. Evidence suggests the men were trying to smuggle a wooden box into the US, but there is no sign of the container, nor any clue as to what could be inside. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.desc: Sam heads to Sudan to help a CIA agent gather evidence of al-Qaeda terrorist training, but Callen is forced to rush to the country too, when one of them is killed while carrying out the mission. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Chris O'Donnell

programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An unpopular woman is accused of killing her wealthy husband - but luckily for her, she has a twin sister to provide her with a seemingly airtight alibi. Robert Vaughn guest stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The hi-tech problem-solvers are caught in a dangerous situation when they try to rescue a group of scientists trapped inside a crumbling biodome designed for use on Mars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The day of Toby and Happy's wedding arrives, but when the team is called to help extinguish a catastrophic tunnel fire, it seems their colleagues might not make it to the ceremony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team members are surprised when Gibbs asks DiNozzo's father to join them in their investigation of a murder committed at the US Naval Academy. Guest starring Robert Wagner

Drama, guest starring TJ Ramini and Michael Nouri, with Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Crime drama, starring Emilia Fox

Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

Meanwhile, Sally bites off more than she can chew, the green-eyed monster in Anna makes an appearance when Beth's cousin takes a shine to David and a multiple sclerosis sufferer requires assistance

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Dennis Waterman and former Corrie actress Georgia Taylor guest star in this special festive edition of the popular rural drama. A retired mountaineer (Waterman) still grieving over his son's death in a climbing accident gets a pleasant surprise when his student daughter (Taylor) returns for the holidays. But an even bigger shock is yet to come - not only is she pregnant, but the father-to-be is the very man her dad blames for her brother's demise. Meanwhile, the local women grow fed up with their menfolk when they arrive back from a booze run the worse for drink, prompting them to hang up their aprons and hand over all the Christmas organisation to the fellas. Beth doesn't know what to think when a patient predicts the new year will bring her and Tom the joy they have longed for, and romance is in the air for David and Anna. Lesley Dunlop, Philip Middlemiss, Kerrie Taylor and Samantha Giles star

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David and Anna have a secret romance, Beth starts to feel paranoid about the amount of time Tom is spending at the factory, and Nathan and Kate both have important decisions to make about their futures. Starring Philip Middlemiss, Lesley Dunlop, Kerrie Taylor, Julian Lewis Jones, Adam Paul Harvey and Jenny Platt

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Learning Napoleon has returned from exile, Sharpe leaves Lucille to look after their farm and returns to service on the Prince of Orange's staff. It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads find themselves in a tight spot after a job turns to disaster in Russia. Penniless and desperate to go home, they visit the British Embassy, where news filters through of a department that allows men to travel the world fixing up British Government property. Oz's criminal past comes back to haunt them and threatens to ruin their chances, but, thankfully, Neville has friends in high places who save the day

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads prepare to start work on the Ambassador's residence in Havana, but get off to a bad start with their new gaffer, who makes it clear he considers them a bunch of cowboys. Wyman catches the eye of a sultry local beauty during a night out, while Oz is captivated by the charms of the Cuban National Ballet's leading lady - and turns to Barry for advice on romance. Tim Healy, Noel Clark, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Feeling somewhat less than merry, Compo, Clegg and Foggy visit a friend in hospital before opening their presents. Meanwhile, Sid, Ivy, Nora and Wally Batty, have their relatives over to dinner. Christmas special from 1978, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie uncovers disturbing information regarding his ancestral line. Tyler's surprising return from university adds to his sense of worry, convincing him something serious is about to happen. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isn't enough room for her family as well. Llewellyn's student helpers disappear, so he hatches a plan to appropriate Boycie's staff and the new arrivals in one fell swoop. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers Marlene has been discussing their marital problems with a counsellor, and the subsequent row results in him being thrown out of the house. With the hotel booked up and his car in a pond, Boycie's only option is to sleep on Bryan's caravan floor. Comedy, starring John Challis and Sue Holderness

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama. Mike and his team are drawn into a world of Victorian dress-up and escapism when they investigate a murder at a Brokenwood steampunk event

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest encounters a man who intends to solve his father's murder a year earlier with the use of his brand-new invention - a time machine. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: World-weary detective inspector John Rebus goes on the trail of a copycat serial killer, only to be taken off the case pending an inquiry into the death of a convicted murderer he helped put behind bars 15 years earlier. Crime drama based on the books by best-selling author Ian Rankin, starring John Hannah, Jim Norton, Ewan Stewart, Sara Stewart and Joanna Roth

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jardine and Reid investigate the murder of a crook who masterminded the theft of a fortune in bullion 15 years previously - half of which was never recovered. Discovering several of his associates have also been bumped off, they deduce someone must be systematically killing the entire gang in a bid to get hold of the remaining loot. Starring James Macpherson and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A party of pilgrims is imprisoned within Shrewsbury Abbey on suspicion of murdering an old man whose body is discovered in a sack. Sleuthing monk Cadfael is soon on the scent of the killer, boiling the corpse down to its bones to ascertain the cause of death - and finding a tiny cross that could provide a vital clue. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Julian Firth and Anthony Green

programme.desc: Father and son Wayne and Connagh Howard visit Welsh islands. Travelling from their home in Cardiff, they begin on Anglesey and also visit Wales's very own 'Love Island', Llanddwyn

programme.title: Cymry ar Gynfas
programme.desc: The return of the show in which famous Welsh people have their portraits painted. In the first show, artist Catrin Williams captures broadcaster Beti George on canvas

programme.title: Cwymp yr Ymerodraethau
programme.desc: Historian Hywel Williams reveals uses certain moments in history to account for the fall of the Ottoman Empire

programme.title: Sgorio
programme.desc: The New Saints v Connah's Quay Nomads (Kick-off 5.15pm). coverage of the JD Cymru Premier encounter at Park Hall

programme.title: Newyddion a Chwaraeon
programme.desc: Weekend news and sport

programme.title: Shane: Torri Record byd Guinness
programme.desc: Wales's record try scorer and former IRB Player of the Year Shane Williams embarks on the biggest cycling challenge of his life - 800 miles across Wales, visiting 50 castles along the way in record time

programme.title: Clwb Rygbi
programme.desc: Ospreys v Cardiff Blues. A chance to see the Raibow Cup encounter at Liberty Stadium

programme.title: Oci Oci Oci
programme.desc: The ladies of Whitland Club, the Tumble Workers Club and Skewen Club compete against each other in the darts quiz, presented by Eleri Siôn and Ifan Jones Evans

programme.title: Standyp: Gwerthu Allan
programme.desc: Master of Ceremonies Dan Thomas introduces an all-woman show. Sarah Breese shares some interesting information, while Jenny Collier shows why she is a rising star in British comedy

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team visits a couple who bought a prefabricated bungalow in Chesterton, Staffordshire, as well as first-time developers in Sutton in Surrey and Bilston in the West Midlands. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell present

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Newbury, Berkshire, with experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper ready to help the blue and red teams maximise their profit at auction. The presenter also recalls the world's largest peace camp

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen intervenes when a bitterly divorced couple arrive for appointments at the same time. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Jan Pearson

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple making an epic location shift, swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Jules also encounters an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the help of expert furniture artist Chloe Kempster to set to work on a reclaimed chest of drawers, while Guy Trench needs to put his best foot forward with some dated shoe lasts. Sarah sets herself the challenge of repurposing some rusty hanging baskets

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary revamps a family treasure with an intriguing history - a threadbare theatre chair that once played host to thousands of theatre-goers and bingo players in the auditorium of the Derby Hippodrome. A rare timepiece that has not worked for years and a cherished bronze locket also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick opens up to Isaac about his brother Paul, and how he wishes he could have opened up about the trouble he was in - the trouble that eventually got him killed. Billy and Honey discover that Amy has been posting nasty things about Janet on social media and Bernie overhears Rainie and Stuart talking about finding a surrogate again

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate from Edinburgh with a virtual audience from Scotland. On the panel are Deputy Leader of the Scottish National Party Keith Brown, Leader of the Scottish Conservative Party Douglas Ross, Leader of the Scottish Labour Party Anas Sarwar, Leader of the Scottish Liberal Democrats Willie Rennie, and Lorna Slater, co-leader of the Scottish Greens

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason discuss the week's biggest stories as they round-up recent events in Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A smart one-bedroom flat in Whitefield in Greater Manchester, a large bungalow bought for an unexpected use in Tipton in Derbyshire and a lovely two-bedroom flat in a Victorian building in London's New Cross are sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell talk to the property developers who hope to make a profit from their buys

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams deal with a crash-for-cash duo when an £8million scam is exposed, and food poisoning leaves one insurer with a claim for eight holiday-makers

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, Stevie and his Special Operations team are called to a car crash in dangerously wet weather. Elsewhere in the city, ambulance crew Johnny and Fraser get a call to a GP surgery where it is reported that a man in his thirties seems to be having a heart attack - highly unusual for someone so young. In Greenock, an elderly woman has fallen at home and needs to be carefully moved and checked by Brian and his Special Operations colleagues. Back in Edinburgh, Moira and Blair attend a man with breathing difficulties who seems to be having a panic attack that's making his symptoms worse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: With the expert help of Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss, contestants battle it out at the Shrewsbury Flea - Antique and Collectors Fair to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also finds out about a local man who revolutionised the postal service

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, a renowned pet-portrait artist, a character-catching ceramicist and an award-winning chainsaw sculptor create portraits of a rescue dog who had a tough start in life

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley goes house hunting with a couple who are looking to return to their Welsh homeland after 20 years of living in Oxford. The pair have a budget of £450,000 to find the right home in Carmarthenshire with enough land to accommodate visiting friends and their pooches. Elsewhere, Ginny samples a regional delicacy at Britain's only manufacturer of pickled cockles

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph heads to Walsall Recycling Centre, where she rescues a bamboo chair, a handmade sideboard and a pair of solid wood bedside tables. With the help of upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and screen print artist Daniel Heath, the tired trio of items have the chance to shine once more

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on a repair of a faded and misshapen wooden memorial board that records the names of all the men of the village of Norton in Northamptonshire who served in the First World War. A 1960s one-armed bandit and a much-loved teddy bear whose lifelong owner has recently died also receive attention. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a guest co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Angela Scanlon and John Simm join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack, hoping to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Isaac cannot get the idea of his misunderstood brother Paul out of his head - and wonders if the doctors have misdiagnosed his schizophrenia. Karen and Bernie return home to find a threatening message from the loan sharks, giving Karen the idea of becoming Rainie and Stuart's surrogate. Nancy is clearly unhappy when Mick asks her to take care of Ollie, while Will steps up to the mark to protect his sister

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and author Emma Dabiri joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Philomena (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1950s Ireland becomes pregnant, and is sent to a home for 'fallen women', while her baby is forcibly taken from her and sent to America to be adopted. Fifty years later, she meets a disillusioned political journalist who attempts to reunite her with her son. Fact-based drama, starring Judi Dench and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Richard Bainbridge and Georgina Hayden are joined by special guest Bill Bailey. Drinks expert Helen McGinn recommends wines via video link

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including West Ham United v Chelsea in the Premier League and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby and Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Amber Gill, Rob Deering, Ben Bailey Smith and Katie Razzall to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - which are How to Train Your Dragon, Stranger Things, Richard Pryor and Dora Maar. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most controversial moves in Premier League history

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer Jacqui Joseph swoops in on an old ottoman, some rusty garden gates and unwanted printer's trays, enlisting upholsterer Sharon O'Connor and blacksmith Kev Paxton to turn them into profit-makers

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris takes a look at nature's best navigators, with mule deer building up complicated memory maps of their landscapes so they don't get lost, while elephants rely on the knowledge of the herd's matriarch to find precious waterholes. Dung beetles plot routes by reading the stars, while the mantis shrimp has one of the most complex navigation systems in the natural world. For many species of fish and birds, there's safety in numbers and they often travel in vast shoals and flocks - but who leads the way, and how do they decide?

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The afternoon session on day six at the Crucible, featuring the conclusion of Shaun Murphy v Mark Davis in round one and the opening frames of Ronnie O'Sullivan v Anthony McGill in round two

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A vintage sewing table and a martial arts expert's karate belts are among the transformations tackled by the team of skilled craftspeople. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Provence in southern France. Starting out in Marseille, they meet a Moroccan woman who runs a welcome centre where immigrants can eat together - and who makes a fish stew to die for. Travelling west, they learn about the culinary influence of the Romans who conquered Europe 2,000 years ago, try a breakfast of pastry filled with spicy octopus, and dine with a Catalan farmer and his family on a regional speciality of barbecued snails

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For their main courses, one chef is showcasing fallow deer in their dish celebrating the invention of cats eyes. Another pays homage to the Humber Bridge, and the final chef pays tribute to a miners' lamp with a dish centred on mutton. Whose inventiveness in the kitchen will veteran judge Simon Rogan rate the highest? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to South America to work in Vira Vira, a hacienda hotel that overlooks an active volcano near Pucon, the adventure capital of Chile. Giles and Monica prepare a meal for guests, go trekking through the Chilean forests on horseback and hear how the chef has an abundance of Chilean produce for his recipes

programme.title: Don Rodolfo
programme.desc: Facing certain death, Don Rodolfo, the world's greatest swordsman, lover and butthead, tells his epic tale. Comedy short based on the hit live show of the same name, starring Ciaran Dowd and Jayde Adams

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Sukh Ojla and Mark Simmons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day six at the Crucible, featuring the concluding session of Mark Selby's first-round match against Kurt Maflin and the start of Neil Robertson v Jack Lisowski in round two

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first and second-round matches on the sixth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the help of expert furniture artist Chloe Kempster to set to work on a reclaimed chest of drawers, while Guy Trench needs to put his best foot forward with some dated shoe lasts. Sarah sets herself the challenge of repurposing some rusty hanging baskets

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention. Others items in need of restoration are an air-powered toy car, an RNLI donation box and a hand-tailored bear used as a Second World War mascot. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A Swiss clock that caused a couple's first argument 40 years ago, and a collection of skateboards in need of a new purpose are among the transformations

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the nation's lochs, discovering the secrets of these iconic features of the Scottish landscape

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. Guest judge Sophie Conran, cook, author and designer, helps decide the winner. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don takes viewers through the next steps of chilli seed growth. Joe Swift gets design inspiration from a courtyard garden, and Nick Bailey finds out about an annoying pest spoiling box hedges

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes, Stacey Solomon, Joe Sugg, Alice Levine and Phil Wang go out for dinner together. They play a series of games, with the loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day seven at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Ronnie O'Sullivan v Anthony McGill

programme.desc: A look back at the second-round matches on the seventh day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Cally is horrified when she discovers the truth about the four Cylons on board Galactica, and takes a drastic course of action. Meanwhile, Lee's political career gets off to an uncertain start, the divisions within the Cylon fleet lead to violence and Boomer suffers from divided loyalties

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Gaius Baltar begins to openly promote belief in the Cylon god, setting off a debate on religious freedom. Colonel Tigh, Galen Tyrol, and Tory Foster argue over how far they should go to ensure their secret is not revealed. Sci-fi adventure, starring James Callis, Michael Hogan, Aaron Douglas and Rekha Sharma

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: Told through candid interviews and personal archive, cameras reveal what happens as lockdowns continue around the planet and the pressure of being trapped indoors begins to be felt. Contributors include a French photojournalist who is forced to decide between working and home-schooling, and heads to the frontline of the pandemic

programme.desc: Team captains Camilla and Noah are joined by Richie Driss from Blue Peter and comedians Jarred Christmas, Marlon Davis and Jenny Bede

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team comes to the aid of nine-year-old Maddie, who has mild cerebral palsy and needs a space of her own where she can start focusing on her sporty future

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The launch of a new eco-competition

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: It is time for a non-seasonal Christmas special, but things go awry when Amelia and her friends accidentally put Santa in a critical condition

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Someone is sabotaging this episode for their own personal gain, and Amelia must find out who before the whole show goes down the televisual toilet

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall challenges a cheetah to a race and meets a cute but deadly bird

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Historian Bettany Hughes investigates the ideas of ancient philosophers, starting with the Indian nobleman Siddhartha Gautama, more popularly known as Buddha. Thought to have been lived and tought between the sixth and fourth centuries BC, the sage and holy man inspired a diverse belief system that influences the lives of millions of people to this day. She travels to India, where the Buddha experienced the challenging ideas and extreme methods of wandering 'truth seekers', after he had abandoned his family and homeland in the Himalayas to embark on his philosophical quest to find a solution to human suffering

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Neil Robertson v Jack Lisowski in the second round, and the second session of John Higgins v Mark Williams or Sam Craigie

programme.desc: Coverage of the annual event, held this year at St Jakobshalle in Basel, Switzerland, featuring day one of the apparatus finals

programme.title: Live Women's Six Nations
programme.desc: England v France or Ireland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Coverage of the final, as Pool A winners England take on the team that finished top of Pool B at a venue to be confirmed. The English were comfortable victors over Scotland and Italy in the pool stage, but will expect a tougher test this afternoon

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby or Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Presenter Sabrina Grant brings in a box of her own precious items for a transformation, a collection of denim clothing that belonged to her sister, who died suddenly 10 years ago

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the seven remaining contestants are challenged to make a chain collar and a sweetheart brooch, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams or Sam Craigie in round two, and the start of Shaun Murphy or Mark Davis v Yan Bingtao. Three-time champion Williams recorded a 13-11 victory over 2015 title-winner Stuart Bingham in round two last year

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: A celebration of the biggest songs from 1987. Performers include Rick Astley, Whitney Houston, T'Pau, Mel & Kim, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Eric B & Rakim, Pet Shop Boys, Alison Moyet and Boy George

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams or Sam Craigie

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the second-round matches on the eighth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day six at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Mark Selby's first-round match against Kurt Maflin and the opening frames of the second-round contest between Neil Robertson and Jack Lisowski. Three-time champion Selby prevailed 10-6 over Jordan Brown in round one last year and went on to reach the semi-finals, where he was eventually defeated 17-16 by Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War

programme.title: The Man with the Iron Heart (2017)
programme.desc: In 1942, two Czech resistance members prepare for a suicide mission against the Third Reich as they plan to assassinate Nazi leader Reinhard Heydrich, the mastermind of the Final Solution. Thriller, starring Jason Clarke, Rosamund Pike, Jack O'Connell and Jack Reynor

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: In a documentary made when she was 105, Brunhilde Pomsel talks about her time as the secretary and stenographer to Nazi propaganda minister Joseph Goebbels. Reflecting on her life, she makes it clear that the dangers that led to the rise of fascism have not been overcome

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox follows up his British Masters series by investigating artists' use of blue, white and gold, and explains how, by choosing these colours, painters have stirred emotions, changed the way people behave and had an impact on the course of history. He begins with gold and its use in early sun worship, before reinterpreting the treasures of the pharaohs, the mosaics of Byzantium and the work of Renaissance master Benvenuto Cellini. He also explores Gustav Klimt's The Kiss and discovers a Birmingham inventor who tried to achieve the alchemist's dream - turning base metals into gold

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day seven at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Ronnie O'Sullivan v Anthony McGill in round two, and the start of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson. Defending champion O'Sullivan will be looking to progress to the quarter-finals this evening, and he came through a difficult encounter against Ding Junhui 13-10 in round two last year

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on December 13. With performances by Shakin' Stevens, Malandra Burrows, Seal, Chris Isaak, Dimples D, the Righteous Brothers, Yazoo, Black Box and Vanilla Ice

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on December 20. With performances by Snap, Cliff Richard, Vanilla Ice, the Carpenters, INXS, Enigma, MC Hammer, Status Quo, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, and John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John

programme.title: St George's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A trip through the archives, showcasing a selection of songs celebrating English history, landmarks and places. This eclectic mix features a huge array of musical styles and tastes, ranging from Elton John to Maddy Prior and Joe Strummer, as well as one of English football's oddest cheerleading combinations in the Spice Girls and Echo and the Bunnymen

programme.title: Definitely Dusty
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the career of Dusty Springfield, the renowned pop diva famous for classic songs such as You Don't Have to Say You Love Me and Son of a Preacher Man. Archive footage shot in both the UK and the US provides an illuminating insight into her glamorous but private personal life, and contributions from her protective inner circle of friends highlight the Dusty behind the panda eyes and blonde beehive. Includes interviews with Tom Jones, Elton John, Burt Bacharach, Lulu and Neil Tennant

programme.title: Kate Bush at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the singer's performances at the BBC's studios between 1978 and 1994. Bush appeared on a variety of programmes, including Top of the Pops, Wogan, Ask Aspel, Saturday Night at the Mill and the Leo Sayer Show. Featuring the hits Wuthering Heights, Babooshka, Running Up That Hill and Hounds of Love, as well as intriguing and lesser-known material

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The creation of the band's second album, which took the gothic synth-pop foundations of the band and combined them with arena-ready anthems leading to critical acclaim and the international hit singles Mothers Talk, Shout and Everybody Wants to Rule the World. With full co-operation of the band members, this documentary explores how the album was recorded, leaving its indelible imprint on New Wave

programme.title: St George's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A trip through the archives, showcasing a selection of songs celebrating English history, landmarks and places. This eclectic mix features a huge array of musical styles and tastes, ranging from Elton John to Maddy Prior and Joe Strummer, as well as one of English football's oddest cheerleading combinations in the Spice Girls and Echo and the Bunnymen

programme.title: Definitely Dusty
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the career of Dusty Springfield, the renowned pop diva famous for classic songs such as You Don't Have to Say You Love Me and Son of a Preacher Man. Archive footage shot in both the UK and the US provides an illuminating insight into her glamorous but private personal life, and contributions from her protective inner circle of friends highlight the Dusty behind the panda eyes and blonde beehive. Includes interviews with Tom Jones, Elton John, Burt Bacharach, Lulu and Neil Tennant

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Life in the treacherous seas that surround the South American wilderness. The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams or Sam Craigie and the start of Shaun Murphy or Mark Davis v Yan Bingtao. The biggest shock of round two last year was unseeded Norwegian Kurt Maflin's 13-11 victory over Scotland's four-time champion Higgins

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: Salvo and Livia have decided to spend some time apart, leaving the inspector gloomy and confused. When 60 security boxes are stolen from a small local bank, Montalbano must seek advice from Stella Parenti, the alluring manager of a rival firm whose romantic advances may dispel his melancholy mood. However, the troubled sleuth's personal dilemmas resurface when he is called to investigate the death of a retired doctor found murdered in his own home. Crime drama, starrng Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Rome's Invisible City
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by Dr Michael Scott to explore the underground treasures that made Rome the powerhouse of the ancient world. Using 3D scanning technology, he uncovers the lost subterranean world of the Colosseum, as well as the aqueducts and sewers that supplied and cleansed it, and helped run civilisation's first metropolis and its empire

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Profile of the enigmatic American musician, who rose to fame in the 1980s with hits including 1999, Kiss and Raspberry Beret. As well as achieving significant commercial success around the world, he won critical acclaim for his adventurous, genre-blurring albums, such as Sign O' the Times and Around the World in a Day - while also making headlines for his sexually explicit lyrics and stage shows, as well as his legal battle to retain control of his name and music. Featuring contributions by guitarist Dez Dickerson, Public Enemy frontman Chuck D, soul singer Beverley Knight and Paisley Park label president Alan Leeds

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Part two of two. Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this latter part of the tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise

The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

This BAFTA winning pre-school observational documentary follows the lives of young children and their families from a variety of backgrounds across the UK.

programme.desc: Funella has written the beginning to a new song to welcome guests into the Furchester Hotel. It's very very catchy. So catchy that it instantly gets stuck in Harvey P Dull's head and it's really getting on his nerves. Furgus suggests that he takes a walk in the garden to clear his head, but that doesn't work. Cookie Monster tries singing him a new song, the Cookie Cookie Yum Yum song but that doesn't work either. Elmo suggests that he tries counting, which works for a little while, but then the song comes back into his head! But the Furchester's can solve any problem and when it seems that Harvey can't take it anymore; Funella announces that she has finished the welcome song! Phoebe gets a monster idea: If they sing the song all the way to the end, then it might end in Harvey's head too. Everyone in the hotel sings the song and at the end Harvey is cured!

They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Jules also encounters an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.title: Newscast

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed

In Greenock, an elderly woman has fallen at home and needs to be carefully moved and checked by Brian and his Special Operations colleagues. Back in Edinburgh, Moira and Blair attend a man with breathing difficulties who seems to be having a panic attack that's making his symptoms worse

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Isaac cannot get the idea of his misunderstood brother Paul out of his head - and wonders if the doctors have misdiagnosed his schizophrenia. Nancy is clearly unhappy when Mick asks her to take care of Ollie, while Will steps up to the mark to protect his sister

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and author Emma Dabiri joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert joins a crew of road workers in south Wales to find out what maintaining the highways and byways is really like. All the signs point to appalling weather, a risky environment and back-breaking labour, but if he can get the team to 'dig' his digging, can road-work-rookie Gilbert bag himself the best seat on the job?

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: Philomena (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1950s Ireland becomes pregnant, and is sent to a home for 'fallen women', while her baby is forcibly taken from her and sent to America to be adopted. Fifty years later, she meets a disillusioned political journalist who attempts to reunite her with her son. Fact-based drama, starring Judi Dench and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including West Ham United v Chelsea in the Premier League and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby and Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.desc: By All for Unity

programme.title: The One Show
