programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick opens up to Isaac about his brother Paul, and how he wishes he could have opened up about the trouble he was in - the trouble that eventually got him killed. Billy and Honey discover that Amy has been posting nasty things about Janet on social media and Bernie overhears Rainie and Stuart talking about finding a surrogate again

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate from Edinburgh with a virtual audience from Scotland. On the panel are Deputy Leader of the Scottish National Party Keith Brown, Leader of the Scottish Conservative Party Douglas Ross, Leader of the Scottish Labour Party Anas Sarwar, Leader of the Scottish Liberal Democrats Willie Rennie, and Lorna Slater, co-leader of the Scottish Greens

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason discuss the week's biggest stories as they round-up recent events in Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A smart one-bedroom flat in Whitefield in Greater Manchester, a large bungalow bought for an unexpected use in Tipton in Derbyshire and a lovely two-bedroom flat in a Victorian building in London's New Cross are sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell talk to the property developers who hope to make a profit from their buys

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams deal with a crash-for-cash duo when an £8million scam is exposed, and food poisoning leaves one insurer with a claim for eight holiday-makers

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, Stevie and his Special Operations team are called to a car crash in dangerously wet weather. Elsewhere in the city, ambulance crew Johnny and Fraser get a call to a GP surgery where it is reported that a man in his thirties seems to be having a heart attack - highly unusual for someone so young. In Greenock, an elderly woman has fallen at home and needs to be carefully moved and checked by Brian and his Special Operations colleagues. Back in Edinburgh, Moira and Blair attend a man with breathing difficulties who seems to be having a panic attack that's making his symptoms worse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: With the expert help of Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss, contestants battle it out at the Shrewsbury Flea - Antique and Collectors Fair to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also finds out about a local man who revolutionised the postal service

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Here, a renowned pet-portrait artist, a character-catching ceramicist and an award-winning chainsaw sculptor create portraits of a rescue dog who had a tough start in life

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley goes house hunting with a couple who are looking to return to their Welsh homeland after 20 years of living in Oxford. The pair have a budget of £450,000 to find the right home in Carmarthenshire with enough land to accommodate visiting friends and their pooches. Elsewhere, Ginny samples a regional delicacy at Britain's only manufacturer of pickled cockles

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph heads to Walsall Recycling Centre, where she rescues a bamboo chair, a handmade sideboard and a pair of solid wood bedside tables. With the help of upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and screen print artist Daniel Heath, the tired trio of items have the chance to shine once more

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on a repair of a faded and misshapen wooden memorial board that records the names of all the men of the village of Norton in Northamptonshire who served in the First World War. A 1960s one-armed bandit and a much-loved teddy bear whose lifelong owner has recently died also receive attention. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Laura Goodwin

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Freedom Alliance

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a guest co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits a spectacular hill fort in Aberdeenshire, discovering the lost landscape of Scotland's ancestors. Anne Lundon finds out why the Flow Country blanket bog is bidding for global recognition as a World Heritage Site and also meets renowned photographer Shahbaz Majeed in his favourite landscape - Glencoe. Euan Mcilwraith discovers the story of the Scottish cattle rancher, while Arlene Stuart marks the centenary of the birth of much-admired landscape artist Joan Eardley

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Isaac cannot get the idea of his misunderstood brother Paul out of his head - and wonders if the doctors have misdiagnosed his schizophrenia. Karen and Bernie return home to find a threatening message from the loan sharks, giving Karen the idea of becoming Rainie and Stuart's surrogate. Nancy is clearly unhappy when Mick asks her to take care of Ollie, while Will steps up to the mark to protect his sister

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and author Emma Dabiri joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Philomena (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1950s Ireland becomes pregnant, and is sent to a home for 'fallen women', while her baby is forcibly taken from her and sent to America to be adopted. Fifty years later, she meets a disillusioned political journalist who attempts to reunite her with her son. Fact-based drama, starring Judi Dench and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Richard Bainbridge and Georgina Hayden are joined by special guest Bill Bailey. Drinks expert Helen McGinn recommends wines via video link

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including West Ham United v Chelsea in the Premier League and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby and Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Amber Gill, Rob Deering, Ben Bailey Smith and Katie Razzall to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - which are How to Train Your Dragon, Stranger Things, Richard Pryor and Dora Maar. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the quarter-finals, as the teams contested places in the Hampden Park semi-finals

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins or Matthew Selt v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby and Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Amber Gill, Rob Deering, Ben Bailey Smith and Katie Razzall to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - which are How to Train Your Dragon, Stranger Things, Richard Pryor and Dora Maar. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most controversial moves in Premier League history

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Wednesday's business in the House of Lords including questions to government ministers, statements on Afghanistan and on the Commission on Race and Ethnic Disparities followed by the consideration of amendments made by the Commons to the Domestic Abuse Bill

programme.title: Northern Ireland Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Commons to the Northern Ireland Secretary Brandon Lewis and his team of ministers, from earlier today.

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions

programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the Church Commissioners, House of Commons Commission, Public Accounts Commission and Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission.

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Clive Myrie investigates allegations of racism in the Church of England. A year after the archbishop of Canterbury, Justin Welby, admitted that the Church was still 'deeply institutionally racist', Clive meets vicars, curates and theological trainees to understand the scale of the problem. He hears stories of racist abuse and claims of a culture that creates a hostile environment for Christians of colour, who have suffered in silence for fear of further discrimination or losing their jobs

programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis in May 2020 sparked a public outcry that swept across America and the world. Nada Tawfik reports on the outcome and the impact of one of the most important trials America has ever witnessed.

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Under cover of Covid, the Amazon rainforest is under attack. Deforestation is at levels not seen for more than a decade. Brazil's president, Jair Bolsonaro, talks of opening the forest up to development while the environmental police are under attack from loggers. Our World has obtained a recording of the environment minister talking about using the cover of Covid to 'change all the rules' in the Amazon. Justin Rowlatt goes on a mission to find out how a tribe he visited a decade ago is faring in the face of this assault.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Everybody is busy preparing for the Grand Concert, which takes place each year. Grandad Island is visiting and everyone is gearing up for a great evening, but things don't go quite as planned.

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: How the flying fish can defend themselves from their predators by flying through the water at speed

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane, Sam, Sid and Mama Polenta are all on a cherry-picking mission as Shane prepares to make a special crumble

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Ela the dragon guards the friends' picnic across the muddy river, as Hari searches for a bridge

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: It is springtime and Rita and Crocodile are trying out their new boots in the muddy puddles

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Martin MacIntyre reads An t-Àite as Fheàrr san t-Saoghal, written and illustrated by Peter Horácek and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: An accidental sidekick swap leaves Danger Mouse with Pinfold, Penfold's cousin

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Someone or something is moving in the cave where Ronia and Biorc are staying. What do they want and how will Ronia and Biorc handle the situation?

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Things go awry with the Competitors but Crùbag is near to hand, with lots of rhythm and the “cha cha cha”

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: The summer has come to an end and Matilda must go back to the capital, moving away from her friends, especially Charlie

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: While out-and-about, Mairi MacRitchie tastes traditional Cornish pasties with crisp pastry and a tender meat and vegetable filling. She later prepares a Battenberg cake

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary looking at high and low sports, including deep sea diving and mountain climbing

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Leugh Mi (Book Show)
programme.desc: Anna Macleod is in conversation with two guests each week about their favourite books. This week, she is joined by Rev Donald Michael MacInnes and Joe Sanders

programme.title: Of Diabolical Purpose - Sgeulachd Monsieur Chantrelle
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of Eugene Chantrelle, a French teacher hanged for murdering his wife in Edinburgh in 1878. A gifted linguist with medical knowledge, he frequently threatened to poison his wife - and was accused of so doing upon her death. Chantrelle pleaded his innocence up to the moment he was hanged and the evidence against him was circumstancial - and his great-grandson speaks of the lasting damage caused to the family

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Ruaridh makes a tasty beer-battered fish, while Uisdean prepares twice-cooked chips to go with it, and then makes Scampi and “Clampi” in tempura batter

programme.title: Bana-Churaidhean/Warrior Women
programme.desc: Docu-drama series on history's most iconic women fighters. This episode follows the life of a fictional Roman gladiatrix called Ardala

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Loch Morlich, a freshwater loch near Aviemore for a frosty swim

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon travels to East Lothian for a weekend of high-octane fun and fascinating history. Starting in Dunbar, Ramsay learns about the humble beginnings of John Muir

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to Glasgow to meet local nursery school pupils and read Ùpraid nan Ròbot written by Sean Taylor and illustrated by Ross Collins, and translated into Gaelic by Norman Campbell

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Oona and Baba want to be of help as the puffin burrow gets a spring clean. After deciding they will collect soft things to make a new bed, the pair take an unexpected adventure

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Famous pop star Brad Lee is due in town, but presently stuck at sea as his tour ship has broken down. Pàdraig is sent to rescue him in the SDS boat, but Brad isn't quite as comfortable on such a small boat

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Mum is going out and now dad has to put Huggleboo to bed.Huggleboo is anything but sleepy

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Shona Masson reads Chan e Gnothaich Famh Beag a Bh’ ann by Werner Holzwarth, illustrated by Wolf Erlbruch and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: A zebra must learn about life on the savannah by following in his father's footsteps, the leader of this particular zebra herd. However, the road ahead is hazardous and Ziggy must travel great distances to find food, evade lions and cross crocodile infested rivers. Will he survive, earn his stripes and be promoted to leader of his own zebra squad?

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: It's party time and the competitors are full of fun and joyfulness, but it's unlikely that the neighbours will approve of the noise

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Ria from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies which include art, swimming and baking

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: A flood leaves the Moomins without a house

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: American fitness enthusiast Arnold Logan is invited to the town to sell his muscle-gain products

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inventions in the 1960s

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod visits Skye and Applecross as he continues his investigation into the history of place names in the Highlands

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar visits Glasgow, where she draws a colour portrait of singer Joy Dunlop

programme.title: The Call of the Isles
programme.desc: Fyfe Robertson presents a journey through some of Scotland's Islands, including Mull, Straffa, Barra and Vatersay. The programme was first broadcast in 1963

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Applecross in Wester Ross, travelling from the edge of the sea to the top of hills to catch that special photo

programme.title: Eadar na Dha: 1918-1935/Clash of Futures: 1918-1935
programme.desc: A look back at Ireland in the aftermath of the First World War, as old conflicts arise over the country's future and other European powers gather for the Paris Peace Conference

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Loch Morlich, a freshwater loch near Aviemore for a frosty swim

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, Co Down, featuring a duet with Mike Denver and his special guest Siobhán Ní Mhaoileoin

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Everybody is busy preparing for the Grand Concert, which takes place each year. Grandad Island is visiting and everyone is gearing up for a great evening, but things don't go quite as planned.

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The starfish tells us how they can stand, walk and eat - even though they don't have any bones to hold them up

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: When the forest friends all hear that the wishing fruit is about to ripen, they all race to be the first to pick one - an achievement that will see them gain a very special power

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Famous pop star Brad Lee is due in town, but presently stuck at sea as his tour ship has broken down. Pàdraig is sent to rescue him in the SDS boat, but Brad isn't quite as comfortable on such a small boat

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: A flood leaves the Moomins without a house

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: There are many tasteful things to behold - except for Coinneach Clogaid

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: The summer has come to an end and Matilda must go back to the capital, moving away from her friends, especially Charlie

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Ria from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies which include art, swimming and baking

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Am BFG (The BFG) (1989)
programme.desc: A friendly giant befriends a lonely little girl and transports her to a strange land. Unfortunately, a number of the other giants there have acquired a taste for eating children. Determined to stop their nefarious habit, the pair set out to charm the Queen of England into putting her armed forces at their disposal. Animated adventure, based on Roald Dahl's classic tale, with the voices of Donald Smith, Catriona Lexy Campbell and Donna Morrison

programme.title: Siubhlachan (Time Traveller)
programme.desc: A girl inherits a watch from her grandfather that allows her to time-travel, but discovers dark forces are intent on taking the device for themselves

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod visits Skye and Applecross as he continues his investigation into the history of place names in the Highlands

programme.title: Gaisgich Oga an Darna Cogaidh
programme.desc: Eva is already an orphan when she arrives in the Auschwitz concentration camp. She hopes to find friends there from the Theresienstadt children's choir

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, with Jim Devine and his special guest Maighréad Ní Dhomhnaill taking to the stage

programme.title: Bana-Churaidhean/Warrior Women
programme.desc: Docu-drama series on history's most iconic women fighters. This episode follows the life of a fictional Roman gladiatrix called Ardala

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum travels across Loch Awe to Kilchurn Castle, looking for the perfect light to take a special photo

programme.title: World Pipe Band Championships
programme.desc: Catrìona MacNeil introduces some of the best bands competing at Grade 1 in the World Pipe Band Championships from previous years

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Sally Lindsay in which a team of charity colleagues answers questions about top 10 lists, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants John, Sylvia, Jack and Dee, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy feels under strain. Ellis confronts Priya and is left red faced. Mandy's actions leave her in grave danger

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: These are unprecedented times, prompting talk of a major transformation for our towns and cities to make them relevant once more in the post-Covid world. But how has the pandemic reshaped our lives? And will our high streets adapt to survive? Jonathan Maitland investigates

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon frets, Mandy is in danger, and Tracy feels put out

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The Ruddock brothers come up against Olympian Colin Jackson and his clan. They'll face a variety of general knowledge-based rounds under the watchful eye of Anne Hegerty, as they attempt to make it through to the semi finals. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity?

programme.title: Accused of Murdering Our Son - The Steven Clark Story
programme.desc: Steven Clark, 23, disappeared in mysterious circumstances following a family walk with his mother on the 28th December, 1992. In September 2020, his parents Doris, 81, and Charles, 78, were arrested on suspicion of murder following a review by police from the Cleveland and North Yorkshire Cold Case Unit. Presenter and ex-detective Mark Williams-Thomas takes a look at the case

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The naked bodies of three men are found placed in the shape of a triangle, leading Jack to suspect the work of a satanic cult. Meanwhile, press baron James Callum's plans to make his mark on Denton are hindered by an investigation into his lover's death in a houseboat fire - and the suspicious demise of his former girlfriend may be the key to unmasking the killer. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Adrian Lukis, Sarah Matravers and Paul Shane

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: McFly's Danny Jones and chef Ainsley Harriott join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is housesitting for Weasel. When he sees him returning from the grocery store with Fizz, he thinks they're burglars!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are at the supermarket buying ingredients to make the world's best cake. They bump into Magoo, who thinks he's at a carnival!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean gets involved in a marrow-growing contest with his neighbour and, as the competition hots up, he tries all sorts of weird and wonderful methods to win. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Grandpa Max's alma mater asks him to use the Omni-Copter to transport an important exhibit to an exotic nature reserve but when the exhibit turns out to be a rare and very large baby moth Ben must keep Tim Buktu from stealing the moth while learning that sometimes you need to use finesse and not force to solve hard problems.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: More and more people are disappearing in Ninjago City, while the ninja scramble to unlock the secrets of the Prime Empire arcade game. They decide to take the game to Cyrus Borg, hoping he can help them decode the programming and release all the players who have been taken

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja's materialize inside Prime Empire and go immediately on the run when they discover a squadron of Red Visors waiting for them!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe and actor and singer Tom Read Wilson join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer and West End star Kimberley Walsh and there are recipes for seabass, sole and a rack of lamb. Guest chefs Elizabeth Haigh and Paul Ainsworth also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by actor and entertainer John Barrowman and prepares a selection of sweets and treats, starting with his cheesy, gooey souffle Welsh rarebit muffins. Former Great British Bake Off semi-finalist Briony May Williams makes a miso caramel and pear chocolate layer cake, while Olly Smith picks a range of drinks that are perfect for an indulgent moment,

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of jumps finale day at Sandown Park, including the 3.05 Celebration Chase and 3.40 bet365 Gold Cup. Plus, flat racing from Leicester and Haydock Park. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The Ruddock brothers come up against Olympian Colin Jackson and his clan. They'll face a variety of general knowledge-based rounds under the watchful eye of Anne Hegerty, as they attempt to make it through to the semi finals. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Dave, Linda, Andrea and Jonathan pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Southampton for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up Stephen Mulhern and Emma Willis to try to uncover the secret talents of mystery performers based only on their appearance and some intriguing clues, with big money up for grabs

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an updated version of Name That Tune, with four contestants trying to identify songs played by a house band in the shortest time possible and have a chance at winning the £25,000 jackpot. Singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor makes a guest appearance at the piano for the Bid-a-Note round, where contestants go head to head to name the tune in as few notes as possible

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes Culture Club frontman Boy George, Great British Bake Off judge Prue Leith, rapper-turned-chef Big Zuu and comedian Joanne McNally to the sofa, while indie-pop band London Grammar chat and perform in the studio

programme.title: Hellboy II: The Golden Army (2008)
programme.desc: The demon superhero, aided by a team from the Bureau of Paranormal Research and Defence, leads the fight against renegade elf Prince Nuada who is intent on reawakening an unstoppable robot army and bringing about the demise of mankind. Action fantasy sequel directed by Guillermo del Toro, starring Ron Perlman, Selma Blair, Doug Jones and Luke Goss

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is housesitting for Weasel. When he sees him returning from the grocery store with Fizz, he thinks they're burglars!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are at the supermarket buying ingredients to make the world's best cake. They bump into Magoo, who thinks he's at a carnival!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean gets involved in a marrow-growing contest with his neighbour and, as the competition hots up, he tries all sorts of weird and wonderful methods to win. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Grandpa Max's alma mater asks him to use the Omni-Copter to transport an important exhibit to an exotic nature reserve but when the exhibit turns out to be a rare and very large baby moth Ben must keep Tim Buktu from stealing the moth while learning that sometimes you need to use finesse and not force to solve hard problems.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: More and more people are disappearing in Ninjago City, while the ninja scramble to unlock the secrets of the Prime Empire arcade game. They decide to take the game to Cyrus Borg, hoping he can help them decode the programming and release all the players who have been taken

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja's materialize inside Prime Empire and go immediately on the run when they discover a squadron of Red Visors waiting for them!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe and actor and singer Tom Read Wilson join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer and West End star Kimberley Walsh and there are recipes for seabass, sole and a rack of lamb. Guest chefs Elizabeth Haigh and Paul Ainsworth also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by actor and entertainer John Barrowman and prepares a selection of sweets and treats, starting with his cheesy, gooey souffle Welsh rarebit muffins. Former Great British Bake Off semi-finalist Briony May Williams makes a miso caramel and pear chocolate layer cake, while Olly Smith picks a range of drinks that are perfect for an indulgent moment,

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of jumps finale day at Sandown Park, including the 3.05 Celebration Chase and 3.40 bet365 Gold Cup. Plus, flat racing from Leicester and Haydock Park. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The Ruddock brothers come up against Olympian Colin Jackson and his clan. They'll face a variety of general knowledge-based rounds under the watchful eye of Anne Hegerty, as they attempt to make it through to the semi finals. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Dave, Linda, Andrea and Jonathan pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Southampton for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up Stephen Mulhern and Emma Willis to try to uncover the secret talents of mystery performers based only on their appearance and some intriguing clues, with big money up for grabs

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an updated version of Name That Tune, with four contestants trying to identify songs played by a house band in the shortest time possible and have a chance at winning the £25,000 jackpot. Singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor makes a guest appearance at the piano for the Bid-a-Note round, where contestants go head to head to name the tune in as few notes as possible

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes Culture Club frontman Boy George, Great British Bake Off judge Prue Leith, rapper-turned-chef Big Zuu and comedian Joanne McNally to the sofa, while indie-pop band London Grammar chat and perform in the studio

programme.title: Hellboy II: The Golden Army (2008)
programme.desc: The demon superhero, aided by a team from the Bureau of Paranormal Research and Defence, leads the fight against renegade elf Prince Nuada who is intent on reawakening an unstoppable robot army and bringing about the demise of mankind. Action fantasy sequel directed by Guillermo del Toro, starring Ron Perlman, Selma Blair, Doug Jones and Luke Goss

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP struggles to come to terms with his new role as a father, having planned to resume his career as a consultant in London before the arrival of his and Louisa's as-yet unnamed son. To make matters worse, he begins to have serious doubts about the competency of his replacement Dr Di Dibbs when she misdiagnoses a patient as well as herself. Martin is then dealt a devastating personal blow after hearing his aunt Joan has died. Starring Martin Clunes, Joanna Scanlan and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP returns to his former post at the surgery after the departure of Dr Dibbs, agreeing to stay until a replacement is found. He persuades a reluctant Louisa to move into his house so he can help raise their son, and suggests they could all move to London together in the future. Martin then delivers the eulogy at his aunt Joan's funeral and speaks to her sister Ruth, who proves to be just as cantankerous as he is. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam are given 10 hours to find a hostage who was taken by two armed men, following a bungled robbery in which an innocent 'have-a-go-hero' was shot dead. When one of the accused is seized, the team is offered a deal - he will tell them the location of the captive in return for immunity from prosecution. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars as uncompromising lawyer Miriam Pescatore

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack eavesdrop on Blakey's medical - and completely misinterpret its findings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum wins a holiday for two in sunny Spain, so Stan, Olive and Arthur compete to do the chores in the hope that she will invite them to accompany her. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Doris Hare

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Faye tells Craig there's no way she's letting him quit his job for her and it's best they finish. Emma assumes Alina made a move on Lucas. Seb tries out a goth look, but Abi assures him that Nina likes him for who he is

programme.title: Mutiny on the Buses (1972)
programme.desc: Chirpy bus driver Stan Butler lands himself in trouble as he struggles to raise enough money to marry his girlfriend, and on top of training his unemployed brother-in-law for a job at the depot he has to contend with an officious new manager. Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Whicher is hired by his one-time political master, former home secretary Edward Shore, to investigate threats made against his son Charles, who has recently returned to London from India with his young family. Shore fears entanglement in a scandal could have devastating repercussions for his career and insists the detective carry out the investigation without involving his former Met colleagues, even after a murder occurs. Whicher's pursuit of justice takes him to the most dangerous corners of the Docklands, but also brings him into contact with a woman who starts to melt his long-frozen heart. Victorian crime drama, starring Paddy Considine, with Nicholas Jones, John Heffernan and Tim Pigott-Smith

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham tells Miss Strickland that he and Rachel will be getting married and moving away. Bernice and Gavin visit Alan in hospital. Scott tells Roy he slept with Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is very surprised to find a well under his house. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A woman's attempt to cool her husband's ardour has unexpected and tragic consequences, while Greengrass takes up an illegal new hobby - cockfighting. Comedy, starring Bill Maynard

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team struggles to understand the involvement of 12-year-old Kaden Blake in the shooting of mother and carer Lia Brown. As a victim of gang culture, the boy's only protection is keeping silent, and by speaking out about the crime, he sets in motion a devastating chain of events. Guest starring Lucy Speed (EastEnders, The Bill), with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: DS Sam Casey, a young officer with a strong sense of justice, is brought in to investigate the shooting of Devlin outside the Old Bailey, and is surprised to learn the gunman was specifically targeting police officers. The case sees the whole team emotionally spent, with the trial itself at risk of being derailed by a politically driven defence lawyer (Colin Salmon) with an axe to grind. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Freema Agyeman and Paul Nicholls, who joins the cast

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The detective is paired with DC Webster, an officer who is struggling to fit in with life in Denton after being seconded from Scotland, with the duo investigating a robbery at a local casino and a hit-and-run incident involving Roger Massie, the son of a local politician, and soon finds that both matters are linked. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.desc: The thief encounters a deposed king-turned-crooked gambler

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur sees an opportunity to cash in when a footballer with a drink and gambling habit goes missing, but selling his story to the paper is not as easy as it seems when there is more than one person interested in his whereabouts. Comedy drama, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Tom Dove and Neil Spooner head to the Extremadura region of Spain, fishing for carp weighing up to 80lbs

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Much and Scarlet have a frightening encounter with a group of lepers, while Robin rescues a noblewoman from bandits - and learns he is related to his deadly enemy Guy of Gisburne. Dorothy Tutin guest stars

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Mysterious events unfold involving a beautiful blonde, and Templar gets behind the wheel of a rally car. Last-ever episode of the vintage adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Spikings finds his life in danger when he investigates the kidnapping of a police informant's son _ and uncovers corruption at the heart of SI-10. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team are called in to help a KGB agent find a defecting Russian scientist who has disappeared with a deadly toxin. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Fishing fanatics Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner attempt to conquer Croatia. They fish two epic venues, including the famous Lake Ontario. The target for the trio this time is to catch a carp of 60 pounds or more

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Melanie Sykes, Heather Small, Andrew Castle and Graeme Le Saux pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at double glazing at a UPVC recycling plant, the ancient art of milling for a Welsh Bara Brith, Sheffield steel for Olympic ice skates and a craftsman making banjos

programme.title: Role Models (2008)
programme.desc: Two irresponsible salesmen find themselves in trouble with the law after crashing their truck. To avoid jail, they agree to undertake community service looking after a couple of troubled kids. However, it soon seems that going to prison would have been the easier option. Comedy, starring Paul Rudd, Seann William Scott and Christopher Mintz-Plasse

However, he has to keep his blossoming sporting career a secret from his overprotective mother. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Kathy Bates, Henry Winkler and Fairuza Balk

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gives shelter to a friend who has broken out of prison, but his act of charity backfires, costing poor old Arthur dearly. Derek Thompson (Casualty) and Sherrie Hewson (Loose Women) feature alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.desc: Part one of two. Niles is shocked when his former wife Maris asks for his help, deciding it is best to keep it a secret from the heavily pregnant and overemotional Daphne. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: When Erin and JB can't fix the doors back onto their solarium, Dick Strawbridge steps in to help them find a solution to their rusted hinges. Another leak is found, but this time from the roof, so they call in some professional climbers to scale their bell tower to find the source of the problem. With wedding season over, Tim and Margreeth launch a foodie weekend. With a professional chef hired to produce the food and some trainee butlers on hand to serve more than 20 guests, the couple can take it easy - apart from foraging for mushrooms, dressing the dining room and fixing a guest's belt. Meanwhile, May and her son Jack get the yoga room ready for the imminent arrival of guests.

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, three rival couples compete to host the best dinner party and win £1,000. Michelle and Laura kick off the competition with a hearty yet elegant menu. On day two Rob and Emma up the ante with locally sourced produce that's 'proper' dinner party food, before Lorenzo and Katie put on generous Italian feast for the final night

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with naturalist Chris Packham in Dictionary Corner, alongside wordsmith Susie Dent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonathan and Stephen have busy lives running their own businesses, and can't wait to buy a holiday home near the glitzy town of Marbella on Spain's Costa del Sol. The couple are looking for an apartment near the beach, where they can chill out with friends while enjoying spectacular sea views on Spain's sunshine coast. With a budget of £250,000 Jean Johansson rises to the challenge, presenting the pair with five property options to consider

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Disaster strikes for smallholders in France, forcing them to diversify in an unusual direction. A couple from Oldham take on a bar renovation in Spain, while two Brits expand their glamping empire in France

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to the Ladbrooke Hotel in the heart of Birmingham, where manager Snehal Lakhani claims he accepts nothing less than perfection. A room with a broken window proves far from flawless for Natasha and Laura, while Danni and Graham are concerned about the proximity of a train line to their bedroom

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a bounty hunter to earn extra money and hires an unlikely assistant in the form of Ned Flanders - who ends up pursuing him on a charge of skipping bail over a trivial offence. Meanwhile, Marge takes a job at an unusual bakery

programme.title: Ackley Bridge
programme.desc: Tahir's tall stories catch up with him when his plan to help Sam backfires disastrously. Kayla decides to keep a secret from Fizza, who has received bad news about her mother. Martin discovers that Johnny has a talent and makes him an offer

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Ste and James decide to make their relationship official, Tony wants to play match maker for Darren and Nancy, and John Paul is still reeling after the shocking news he received

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Lonesome teenager Jasmine seeks a canine companion to replace her twin sister Shola, who's leaving her to start a university course. Her family thinks that pedigree beagle Zoe will make the perfect new sibling, but Jasmine isn't so sure. Elsewhere, staff of Wood Green animal charity believe Jim the poodle could prove the perfect match for 12-year-old Lexi, who is looking for a 'bedtime buddy'

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall continue their quest to curry favour with omnipotent Taskmaster Greg Davies. Jamali decorates the caravan, Sarah is appalled by a kiwi fruit and, under severe pressure, Mike delivers a revelation so visceral that it leaves everyone in a state of horrified delirium. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Frank's mother Mary is bed-bound for the day, so it falls to him - and therefore Doofus - to complete her list of chores and nurse her back to health. The pair set about their tasks with enthusiasm, stupidly giving Mary sleeping pills so that they can finish their tasks in peace. However, when they return home after a stressful trip to the laundrette, they discover that Mary has escaped. Comedy, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show with a difference, in which another two singletons take their pick of potential partners based purely on their naked bodies. First up is 27-year-old Kelly, a carer from Brighton. She's unsure whether she's looking for a man or a woman but hopes to find her the perfect partner to give her a little TLC. She is followed by 29-year-old steeplejack Carlo from Somerset, who is looking for woman that he can sweep off her feet

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix sets two chefs to the near-impossible challenge of replicating a breakfast favourite - a bowl of Shreddies. Daniel Clifford and Claude Bosi both hold two Michelin stars. Both have also previously tried and failed to win a Snackmasters trophy, so this extremely difficult challenge is their chance to earn redemption. They have three weeks to work out the factory processes that give Shreddies their famous lattice pattern and layered structure, before a cook-off where the factory judges will decide the winner

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones is joined by fellow comedian Joe Wilkinson for a traditional seaside adventure in the North Yorkshire coastal town of Whitby, a place that boasts history, gothic architecture and the chippie that won the award for the UK's best fish and chips. The pair explore the area's famed Jurassic Coast where, alongside a fossil-hunting expert, they attempt to find ancient relics on the beach. In homage to the area's association with Dracula, the duo also enjoy a full gothic makeover. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Wilkinsburg in Pennsylvania, home to Ms Jean's Southern Cuisine, which was opened by retired special-education teacher Jean Gould. During the chef's attempt to save the business from disaster, he discovers her soul-food restaurant resembles a dentist's waiting room and finds a lot of tension between Jean and her employees, who claim she is a Jekyll-and-Hyde character

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples host dinner parties in the Spanish resort of Marbella as they competes for the cash prize of £1,000. Stephen and his Colombian boyfriend Juan plan to keep things simple in the kitchen, but two of their guests point out that the chicken is overcooked. At Glenn and Henriette's Mexican evening, the contestants get competitive when they're let loose on a pinata, and Tristan and Louise hope to inject love into their dinner party - until Stephen gives them frank feedback on the food

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Spaniel Scrumpy is suffering from lameness and Noel discovers he has a collapsed disc and severe elbow arthritis. He already has a chequered medical history totalling six previous operations to his elbows, hip and knee. Meanwhile, there is an emergency admission when a 15-month-old dachshund arrives with a mystery brain condition, and Noel and his neurology colleagues have their work cut out to identify the cause. When his condition rapidly deteriorates overnight, suddenly the life of this much-loved young dog hangs in the balance

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp uses origami to create the letter A for her son Archie's name sign. She also embroiders a family photo onto some dyed fabric and talks online to crafting experts

programme.title: The £100k Drop
