programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is taught how to bind books by expert Isabelle Ting, and reveals how to create a notebook from leftover materials including leather offcuts, recycled leather coats and decorative paper

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game where contestants pit their wits against the clock. Nicki Chapman is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam regrets letting Diane talk him into asking Norm to sort out his taxes. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and George Wendt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Diane grow tired of Coach hanging around them all the time, so they set him up on a date. Starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra's divorced parents join the Barones for Thanksgiving - but her father puts a damper on the festivities by bringing a date. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton, Brad Garrett and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert encounters a beautiful woman who learns his surname is Barone and mistakenly assumes he is Raymond - a situation he is quick to turn to his romantic advantage. Brad Garrett stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank catches a football at a college game and refuses to give it back. He tries instead to auction it over the radio for $10,000 - much to the horror of Ray, who tries to make him feel guilty for being so mercenary

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles is suspected of being an accomplice to murder when his former wife Maris uses his crossbow to kill her husband - and Frasier does not help matters by accidentally suggesting his brother should receive the death penalty

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Lilith and Frasier are set up on a blind date by a mutual friend, while Martin's gun goes off in the apartment - leaving Niles and Daphne to conceal the damage. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, Bebe Neuwirth and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Maris murder case makes Niles a celebrity, but it's not long before a jealous Frasier seizes the chance to regain his place in the spotlight. Meanwhile, Roz's arrogant sister Denise gets her comeuppance. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB launch their chateau business by opening it up for a tea party for the locals, including the mayor. May holds her health and wellbeing retreat using her new yoga studio for the first time

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three rival duos compete in Cardiff to host the best dinner party. Newlyweds Laura and Sean kick off the week with a Hawaiian feast. Then, Lesley and Keith host, with Lesley wanting to glam things up a bit with some posh nosh. Howevwer, their starter incites some pungent punditing. Laura wows the table with a wedding rendition, and the tale of the missing finger points to Keith. On the final night, everyone is looking forward to Rachel and Lee's curry, but they confound expectations by fusing traditional with different flavours

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with naturalist Chris Packham in Dictionary Corner, alongside wordsmith Susie Dent

programme.desc: James first visited Crete over 40 years ago and has always longed to buy a holiday home there. Now that his partner Alison has also fallen in love with the Greek island too, they've both decided to turn their dream into a reality. Ben Hillman helps the couple find a suitable holiday home - ideally a traditional village house - on their budget of £80,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the summer nears its end, two Brits who own a French campsite find out if they've made enough to keep their dream alive. There are teething problems for a couple who run a bar in Spain. Plus, a couple from Yorkshire tempt French locals with their new Thai takeaway business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, Churchill's Hotel's Jeff Brown and Vicky O'Loughlin are first to face the feedback and Jeff's keen to turn down the heating woes. Plus, Snehal from the Ladbrooke Hotel has a bone to pick with all of the B&Bers. After some frank discussions, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Channel 4 News

Kate Quilton finds out how much milk goes into a bar of chocolate, while Andi Oliver uncovers the reason why asparagus delivers some of its eaters with very pungent urine

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones takes fellow comedian and broadcaster Jamali Maddix to the East Anglian city of Norwich. The pair learn an 18th-century folk dance from a bunch of local men who are keeping the tradition of molly dancing alive, tour the city's epic cathedral and watch tiny trains go round and round at the UK's largest indoor model attraction. The duo also taste a variety of wines at a nearby vineyard and sober up in time to attend a blacksmithing course, during which they heat and hammer metal under the guidance of a local artisan. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Central Intelligence (2016)
programme.desc: Action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson as a one-time geek who suffered constant humiliation at high school but grew up to be a lethal CIA agent. Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Frank's mother Mary is bed-bound for the day, so it falls to him - and therefore Doofus - to complete her list of chores and nurse her back to health. The pair set about their tasks with enthusiasm, stupidly giving Mary sleeping pills so that they can finish their tasks in peace. However, when they return home after a stressful trip to the laundrette, they discover that Mary has escaped. Comedy, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Norwich is hosted by hotel events manager Jo-Anne, who hopes a glamorous gourmet affair will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Down-to-earth multimedia manager Karena hosts the second night in Norwich, where she hopes to impress her guests with some slightly more refined pub-style food, despite questionable table manners and hairy fish during the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional skier Jake hosts the third dinner party in Norwich, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a menu of fine dining and alpine classics. With the help of his mother, he decorates his house to create a winter wonderland, but the games he wants to play leave some guests feeling as if they are at a children's party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music shop manager Barnaby takes the reins of the fourth night in Norwich, and after a fish-inspired starter and a main course of seafood linguine, his guests appear to be getting into the party spirit. However, it seems his only chance of scooping the prize money rests with his entertainment after ending up with a huge pile of leftovers - and a bleeding finger

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Norwich and the turn of business advisor Richard to host. He believes his Thai cooking will give him the edge in the competition, but has his work cut out as Jo-Anne is out to test his ego. Meanwhile, punk rocker Richard feels the spirit of rebellion and hopes to claim the cash prize for himself before the winner is announced

programme.title: Coach Trip

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike becomes extremely vigilant about crime after his wallet is stolen from the dashboard of his squad car while he is taking a nap

programme.desc: Joyce reveals that she intends to leave the house to Victoria in her will, leaving Molly feeling shocked and disappointed

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie lets Arthur decide what meals they eat - so Doug manipulates him into choosing his favourites. Meanwhile, Spence camps out in the queue for the premiere of a new film

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug breaks the double bed, so he and Carrie borrow two singles to tide them over until a replacement is delivered. To their surprise, however, they discover the different sleeping arrangement gives their marriage a much-needed boost

programme.desc: Howard is put in charge of looking after Stephen Hawking's wheelchair when the famous scientist comes to lecture at the university for a week - and takes advantage of his position to blackmail Sheldon into performing assorted unpleasant tasks. Guest starring Stephen Hawking

programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon and Raj organise a stag party for Howard, but stories about his sexual history are unwittingly revealed, and he has to persuade bride-to-be Bernadette not to call off the wedding. Comedy, starring Simon Helberg and Kunal Nayyar

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard tries to use his wedding as an excuse to avoid being sent into space, but ends up accepting the mission. Amy plots to get Sheldon to take the next step in their relationship, while Leonard accidentally asks Penny to marry him

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: As Howard and his fellow astronauts sit in the space shuttle awaiting launch, he reflects on the tumultuous events of the previous few days, when he and Bernadette tried to get married. Meanwhile, his friends witness the blast-off on TV at home

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart becomes addicted to junk food and eventually suffers a minor heart attack. Marge and Homer resort to drastic action and send him to a high-security fat camp - which they fund by taking in German lodgers

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa enters a singing competition on the Krusty the Clown Show and makes it through to the final stages, but Homer's competitive streak threatens to sabotage her performance. Guest starring American Idol 2004 winner Fantasia Barrino as Lisa's rival Clarissa Wellington

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer watches a film about the apocalypse and becomes convinced the end of the world is imminent. Panic-stricken, he begins urging the people of Springfield to join him in preparing to meet their maker - but his dire predictions prove wildly inaccurate

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart becomes convinced Milhouse and Martin are trying to steal a valuable comic book they all clubbed together to buy. As his paranoia escalates, the three enter into a dangerous confrontation that threatens to end their friendship. With the guest voice of Daniel Stern

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Berkshire, beauty therapist Emma and her boyfriend Sean kick off the week with an night of acronyms, pet names and fine food. Finally, Mel and Tony host with a menu filled with traditional British classics in a bid to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bob's in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. Every year profits nosedive through the bleak winter months, and matters aren't improved when it becomes clear they have both lost their passion - not only for the business but also for each other. With the restaurant and marriage on the line, Gordon tries to help turn things around before it's too late

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Vets Emily and Will need to relocate two cheetahs to spread blood lines. Volunteer Debbie is put to the test as nine orphaned baby monkeys arrive at Riverside Rehabilitation Centre

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Farmer's daughter Mandy Sowray and her friend Anny Harrison host first at Laskill Country House in Helmsley, North Yorkshire. Chris Witton and Saroop Ram from the Swan Hotel feel right at home, but tech-savvy Colin and Ben Warburg from Scalford Hall Hotel fail to connect from the start. During the afternoon's hay-bale race, youngsters Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker from the Abbey Hotel fail to stack up to the challenge. At dinner, Mandy bigs up her banger qualifications, but at breakfast she fails to crack Chris's order. Colin's feedback leaves unanswered questions for Mandy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Witton and his spiritual friend Rammy hope that straightforward value for money will win them the competition, as they host at the Swan Hotel in Telford. A Yorkshire pudding-making competition leaves confident cooks Mandy and Anny thinking there's more to youngsters Charlie and Lauren than meets the eye. At dinner, Rammy opens up about his troubled past. Colin has a rude awakening in the morning. At feedback time, Chris has a change of heart about some of his guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Colin Warburg and his student son Ben hope that their 81-room country pile - Scalford Hall Hotel in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire - will wow their rivals. However, Chris and Rammy are still smarting from their feedback and think that Colin is playing games. After an afternoon crate stacking competition on the hotel's high wire course, Colin gets a grilling at dinner. A lukewarm reaction to breakfast only adds to Colin's woes and when he sees his feedback he suspects that retribution is at hand

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Abbey Hotel in Battle, East Sussex, run by confident young colleagues Charlie Thompson and Lauren Tucker. However when Charlie and Lauren's guests arrive, Colin and Ben feel the squeeze and Chris and Rammy find themselves in a sticky situation. On an afternoon at the archery range, scores are settled. At dinner, straight-talking Chris can't help but be impressed, but when the conversation turns to parking, the guests are in a jam with their hosts. Later, Colin's feedback leaves Lauren wanting answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up to settle some scores. Laskill Country House host Mandy wants to crack where she went wrong with breakfast and why Colin and Ben failed to connect with her, while Chris and Rammy from The Swan Hotel wish to question Lauren on her room flaws and get to the bottom of Colin's bedroom nightmares

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up home owners they have previously met on the programme to see how they have got on since they helped them make their decisions. In this episode, Phil catches up with Kathleen and Tom who chose to renovate their three-bedroom Victorian terrace in Gosforth, Newcastle

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: After a four year stint living in New Zealand, ex-RAF pilot Jon and GP Gill Flewers want to build a Kiwi-style hill house on the slopes of the Malvern hills in Worcestershire. They plan to build an ambitious three-storey home clad in wood and stone, but the pair encounter problems as the site is so steep builders do not want to take on the job

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: VE Day in Colour: Britain's Biggest Party
programme.desc: Using newly recorded accounts and previously unseen archive, this is the story of the day in May 1945 when the fighting finally stopped across Europe and the celebrations began as Britain rejoiced in unity. The stories of ordinary folk are heard, but alongside the happy tales of street parties, fancy dress, and jelly and ice cream are the reflections of those thinking of loved ones who wouldn't return to enjoy a peace that was hard won

programme.title: The Great British Spitfire Restoration
programme.desc: Documentary following a team of engineers as they work to restore a 1943 Spitfire to its former glory. Led by a factory owner and operating out of a little-known workshop on one of Britain's most important wartime airbases, the dedicated enthusiasts spend a year getting the iconic aircraft into condition to take to the skies once again. Narrated by Al Murray

programme.title: War for the Planet of the Apes (2017)

Superhero adventure, starring Miles Teller, Michael B Jordan, Kate Mara and Jamie Bell

Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes recalls eight pivotal days that defined the Roman Empire and its establishment as the world's first superpower. She begins by exploring the day in 202BC when Rome defeated the might of Carthage under Hannibal at the Battle of Zama in modern-day Tunisia. This was a decisive moment, setting Rome on the path to greatness and exemplifying the military muscle and supreme ambition on which its empire would be built. Dramatisations featuring Adam Levy, James Sutton and Paul Brennen bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow reveals how the Renaissance movement found a new centre in Venice and examines the life and work of Tiziano Vecellio, known to English-speakers as Titian, who pioneered the use of oils with a radical approach to light, colour and brush strokes. His talent was recognised across Europe and he painted portraits of some of the 16th century's most powerful figures, including Pope Paul III and Charles V of Spain

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: It is 1945, and Winston Churchill has led Britain to the brink of victory, but he is determined to carry on as prime minister and calls a general election. He fully expects voters to give him another five years in Number 10, but he is in for a shock. Once the election is called the tide begins to turn, and Churchill struggles on the campaign trail

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs

Series regulars Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini and Jenny Powell are joined by Carol Decker, who reflects on the era that her band, T'Pau, finally made their breakthrough. It was also a time when Rick Astley, the Firm, Mel & Kim, and Bruce Willis had their share of chart success

programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes looks at the day in 73BC that Spartacus, a Thracian gladiator fighting for the entertainment of the Romans, broke out of gladiator school and started a slave revolt. The Republic's rulers were so panicked by the protest that they offered unprecedented power to a single, ambitious individual - Crassus - who promised victory in what would prove a dark foreshadowing of Rome's slide into dictatorship. Dramatisations featuring Joseph Millson, Adam Basil and Rhydian Jones bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: The life and work of Pieter Bruegel the Elder, generally considered the greatest Flemish painter of the 16th century, whose works remain among the most distinctive examples of Renaissance art, combining gritty humour with political comment. Tim Marlow reveals how Bruegel was influenced by the period of immense social change in which he lived

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gives Chandler a hideous bracelet as a sign of their friendship, while Rachel insists she has no time for Ross - until Monica's old prom video helps her see him in a different light. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston, Courteney Cox and David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel finally consummate their relationship, and it seems romance is also on the horizon for Monica - but the object of her affections happens to be old enough to be her father. With guest star Tom Selleck

programme.title: Live Gallagher Premiership Rugby Union
programme.desc: London Irish v Harlequins (Kick-off 12.30pm). All the action from the top-flight encounter at Community Stadium. Both teams are looking to qualify for the play-offs by finishing in the top four and the sides could not be separated in January's reverse fixture at Twickenham Stoop, with a thrilling match ending 27-27 after Oli Hoskins' late try for the Exiles was converted by Paddy Jackson

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor goes on rail journeys around the globe, jumping on and off trains to find adventure and face challenges, while exploring the history and culture of each place he visits

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small (1975)
programme.desc: A new apprentice to an eccentric vet in the Yorkshire Dales quickly discovers life in the country is not as idyllic as he thought. However, as he finds his feet, the newcomer begins to romance the daughter of a local farmer. Drama based on the series of books by James Herriot, starring Simon Ward, Anthony Hopkins, Lisa Harrow, Freddie Jones and TP McKenna

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small (1975)
programme.desc: A new apprentice to an eccentric vet in the Yorkshire Dales quickly discovers life in the country is not as idyllic as he thought. However, as he finds his feet, the newcomer begins to romance the daughter of a local farmer. Drama based on the series of books by James Herriot, starring Simon Ward, Anthony Hopkins, Lisa Harrow, Freddie Jones and TP McKenna

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Julian treats a seriously ill llama called Echo who is struggling to breathe, and he has the tricky job of conducting his very first llama chest X-ray. Elsewhere, Walter the pug arrives at Huddersfield hospital with a painful knee problem, so Matt and Martin put him through the CT scanner as they try to work out what is causing the pain

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Susan and her campervan Helen arrive on home turf, touring the breath-taking southern Highlands of Scotland on a 140-mile trek from Glasgow to Aberfoyle. At Loch Lomond, the comedian takes to a kayak and hits the water to get a unique view of the amazing local scenery. She visits the spectacular Falls of Dochart, where she gets special access to the stunningly beautiful island of Inchbuie. She also views a bizarre Iron Age-style dwelling, known as a crannog, erected on stilts in Loch Tay

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: Clive decides to introduce a sheepdog puppy to the flock for the first time, and Miles and Sidney take on more responsibilities on the farm while Reuben is busy working full time at his apprenticeship. Disappointed to miss the renowned annual Muker Fell race, Clive and Amanda decide to host their very own competition on home turf, across three miles of Ravenseat's toughest moorland

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: Day-to-day life in Caesars Palace, a hotel themed on ancient Rome and containing numerous restaurants and casinos, which has been the heart and soul of Las Vegas for more than 50 years. Cameras follow the staff who keep make this 4,000-room hotel running 24 hours a day, go behind scenes of a high roller's secret luxury villa and reveal how the needs of 3,000 people are catered for per day. Plus, the secrets of the casino floor are revealed as a young couple tie the knot Vegas-style

programme.title: Edward VII: The Merry Monarch
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the king's life and reign, looking at the relationships with the women in his life and considering the remarkable period in history he presided over - an era when the arts, culture, technology and architecture flourished in Britain

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Comedy
programme.desc: Jo Brand presents a celebration of great sketches from the decade, featuring clips from The Fast Show, Goodness Gracious Me and The Real McCoy. Charlie Higson reveals which memorable Fast Show sketch Paul Whitehouse hated, while the cast of Goodness Gracious Me recall their famous Going for an English sketch

programme.title: Paddy McGuinness: Saturday Night Live
programme.desc: Best known as the host of Take Me Out, Paddy McGuinness rose to fame playing the doorman in Peter Kay's hit Channel 4 comedy Phoenix Nights and its spin-off Max and Paddy's Road to Nowhere. This is a recording of his performance at Manchester Arena during his 2011 UK tour, a show filled with his fast-paced humour and anecdotes, with Paddy injecting a party atmosphere as he holds forth on topics including strip clubs, stag dos and hen nights and invites people from the audience to take part in a version of his ITV dating show

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica's parents are dismayed to discover she is dating their middle-aged friend, and an argument with Chandler prompts Joey to move out of the flat. Matthew Perry and Matt LeBlanc star, with guest appearances by Tom Selleck and Elliott Gould

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel begins to feel smothered by Ross's constant attention. Meanwhile, Phoebe makes a video for her song Smellycat, and Chandler's new flatmate Eddie causes problems

programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes looks at the day in 49BC that Julius Caesar led his army across the River Rubicon. By doing so he ignored the orders of the Roman Senate, and effectively declared war on his rivals in Rome. In time, it would prove a fatal blow to the Republic - the system of elected officials that had governed for nearly half a millennium. Hughes analyses Caesar's character and reveals how new theories about his health shed light on his decision-making, while archeological finds recently dredged from a Dutch river reveal the true genocidal horror of his conquest of Gaul. Dramatisations featuring Jack Ellis, Nathan Dean Williams and Bashar Rahal bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Hell Is for Heroes (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious soldier defies orders to lead two men on a secret attack on a German pillbox, but his mission fails and the other two soldiers are killed. Court-martialled for his actions, he remains convinced his plan was sound and ignores the sentence to make a second attempt at destroying the emplacement. Second World War adventure, with Steve McQueen, James Coburn and Bobby Darin

programme.title: Gunsmoke (1953)
programme.desc: A wicked landowner wants to get his hands on a farmer's ranch, so hires a gunslinger to kill him. However, the mercenary befriends his intended target - and also falls for his daughter - so resolves to defend them from their enemies during a cattle drive. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Paul Kelly, Susan Cabot and Donald Randolph

programme.title: Elephant Walk (1954)
programme.desc: A woman struggles to come to terms with her tea plantation owner husband's obsession with his dead father. Melodrama, starring Elizabeth Taylor, Peter Finch, Dana Andrews and Abraham Sofaer

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly (1966)
programme.desc: A cynical, mercenary cattle drover sets out to deliver a herd to soldiers at a Union base during the American Civil War. During the journey, he is kidnapped by a Confederate colonel, who hopes to persuade him to help steal the animals and take them deep into rebel territory. Western, starring William Holden, Richard Widmark and Janice Rule

programme.title: A Walk in the Woods (2015)
programme.desc: A travel writer decides to hike the Appalachian Trail, a 2,200-mile journey through some of America's most rugged terrain. However, the only companion he can find for his exhibition is a roguish old friend, who sees it as an opportunity to get out of paying his debts. Fact-based drama adapted from Bill Bryson's travel book, starring Robert Redford, Nick Nolte and Emma Thompson

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: Escape from New York (1981)
programme.desc: Former special forces soldier Snake Plissken is sentenced to life on Manhattan Island, an anarchic penal colony for the most dangerous criminals. When Air Force One is hijacked and crashes there, Snake is coerced into rescuing the US president from prisoners holding him hostage. John Carpenter's sci-fi action adventure, with Kurt Russell, Donald Pleasence, Lee Van Cleef, Adrienne Barbeau and Isaac Hayes

programme.title: An Unsuitable Job for a Woman (1981)
programme.desc: A female detective hired to investigate the suicide of a wealthy businessman's son slowly becomes obsessed with the dead youth. Thriller, starring Billie Whitelaw, Paul Freeman, Pippa Guard and Dominic Guard

programme.title: Last Train from Gun Hill (1959)
programme.desc: A marshal discovers the man responsible for raping and killing his wife is actually the son of an old friend, setting the scene for a showdown. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Anthony Quinn, Brian Hutton and Carolyn Jones

programme.title: The Rare Breed (1966)
programme.desc: An English widow decides to fulfil her cattle farmer husband's final wish of introducing the Hereford breed to America. She finds a cattleman in Texas willing to help, but first she and her daughter must transport their prize bull across the wilderness, with the aid of a sceptical ranch hand. Romantic comedy Western, starring Maureen O'Hara and James Stewart

programme.title: The Last Blitzkrieg (1959)
programme.desc: The son of a Nazi general is selected for a secret undercover mission. He and his men disguise themselves as American soldiers and go behind the Allied lines, with the intention of carrying out acts of sabotage and undermining enemy morale. Second World War drama, starring Van Johnson and Kerwin Mathews

programme.title: The Black Shield of Falworth (1954)
programme.desc: The son of a disgraced medieval nobleman is raised in poverty. With the support of a kindly earl, he gains the chance to train as a knight and reclaim his birthright, proving his worth by fighting to foil a plot to overthrow the king. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, David Farrar and Barbara Rush

programme.title: Warcraft (2016)
programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster

programme.title: The Girl in the Spider's Web (2018)
programme.desc: Swedish hacker Lisbeth Salander is hired by computer genius Frans Balder to retrieve Firefall, a programme capable of accessing the world's nuclear codes that he developed for the National Security Agency. Though Salander manages to find Firefall, it's not long before she's fighting for her life against mercenaries. Thriller, starring Claire Foy and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Mother! (2017)
programme.desc: A couple's relationship is tested when uninvited guests arrive at their home, disrupting their peaceful existence. Drama, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Javier Bardem, Ed Harris and Michelle Pfeiffer

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs

programme.title: Madagascar (2005)
programme.desc: A gang of streetwise penguins decides to escape from New York's Central Park Zoo and return to a life in the wild, but when Marty the zebra and his friends follow suit, they end up stranded in Africa on a jungle island without a clue about how to fend for themselves. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ben Stiller, David Schwimmer, Chris Rock, Jada Pinkett Smith and Sacha Baron Cohen

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events (2004)
programme.desc: Three orphans are entrusted into the care of their sinister uncle, a master of disguise who intends to kill them for the family fortune. Quickly realising he is up to no good, the children flee, moving from one prospective guardian to the next - but their wily relative is never far behind. Comedy adventure based on the children's books, starring Jim Carrey, Meryl Streep, Jude Law, Liam Aitken and Emily Browning, with Kara and Shelby Hoffman

programme.title: Billion Dollar Brain (1967)
programme.desc: Former secret agent Harry Palmer comes out of retirement to take a mysterious canister to Finland, and ends up embroiled in an American megalomaniac's bid to take over the world. Espionage thriller, directed by Ken Russell and starring Michael Caine, Oscar Homolka and Karl Malden

programme.title: Goodbye Christopher Robin (2017)
programme.desc: Biopic of children's author AA Milne, focusing on how his relationship with his son influenced the creation of the Winnie the Pooh stories. The books prove immensely popular in a nation recovering from the trauma of the First World War, but bring the youngster who inspired them uncomfortably into the public eye. Starring Domhnall Gleeson, Margot Robbie and Kelly Macdonald

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection (2016)

programme.desc: Famous faces inlcuding Shaun Ryder and Bez, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mother Linda, Denise Van Outen and her boyfriend Eddie Boxshall, and Oti Mabuse and her sisters Motsi and Phemelo give their opinions on This Morning, Killing Eve, Extreme Tribe: The Last Pygmies, Taken, The Chase, 8 Days: To the Moon and Back, and Serengeti

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated viewers the Siddiquis, Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, and Amira and Iqra look back on the year's biggest and best shows, including Strictly Come Dancing, Countdown, This Morning, Yorkshire Tea vs PG Tips: Battle of the Brews, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat, CBeebies Bedtime Stories, The Undoing, First Dates, Borat Subsequent Moviefilm, Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?, Elephant, The Great British Bake Off, The Crown and Naked Attraction

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singletons Jack and Kim, who both enjoy the high life, arrive at the restaurant, while East Ender Terry and hairdresser Levana share a funny and unforgettable date, as they muse on Towie and the merits of keeping an emergency bottle of wine in your wardrobe. Plus, 47-year-old Maria meets 49-year-old pilot and fellow divorcee Paul

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Receptionist Kirsty has high expectations for her date with Oliver and wastes no time in telling him that she wants marriage and kids very soon. DJ Tom struggles with confidence away from his decks and is matched with classics student Olivia, who has never had a date before

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated viewers the Siddiquis, Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, and Amira and Iqra look back on the year's biggest and best shows, including Strictly Come Dancing, Countdown, This Morning, Yorkshire Tea vs PG Tips: Battle of the Brews, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat, CBeebies Bedtime Stories, The Undoing, First Dates, Borat Subsequent Moviefilm, Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?, Elephant, The Great British Bake Off, The Crown and Naked Attraction

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Competitive adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high on his big day and has an adventurous plan to take his wedding to the sky. However, his straight-talking bride-to-be Lynsey is much more down to earth and has a very clear idea of the modest and intimate wedding she wants. While Mark aims to go above and beyond to make his wedding First Class, no nonsense Lynsey just hopes he is thinking about her first and foremost

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Carol and Stephen Howell from Bristol have been going to Barbados on holiday for years and now, with retirement fast approaching, hav decided it's time to buy a holiday home in their dream location. With a budget of £275,000 Jasmine Harman helps them to find their perfect property, whether it be a stunning hillside development or beachfront apartment

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon O'Brien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detective team searches for an heir to the estate of a woman who led a glamorous life of international travel and adventure. There is the story of an East End couple who chose to raise their disabled daughter at a time when it was against conventional wisdom, plus details of unclaimed estates still awaiting heirs

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Wymeswold, Leicestershire, to meet Ally and James, a fearless couple who have taken a gamble buying a tricky site that has eaten up almost all of their cash before work has even begun. As the months turn to years, can they hold their nerve to build the country cottage of their dreams?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Trecarne House in Liskeard, Cornwall, where owners Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The pair win the group's admiration by sharing their life story, but one guest stirs up intrigue by holding his cards close to his chest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners spend their second night of the competition at the Magna Carta at Windsor in Berkshire, a five-star floating bed and breakfast run by Captain Dominic Read. The guests are pleasantly surprised upon arrival, but not all are impressed by the standard of the accommodation - and a cracked teapot at the breakfast table leads to trouble for the proprietor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is Ash Cottage near Leeds Castle in Kent, where the unusual decor is an eye-opener for the guests but is not everyone's cup of tea. Trish and Tom get heated over the instant coffee before owners Rayne and Chris Deal take their fellow contestants to Chatham Dockyard to try their hands at rope-making

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is A Room with a View in Brighton, where owner Stephen Bull and housekeeper Joanna Harris hope their nine-room establishment will impress the contestants. Things get off to a good start when Rayne and Chris Deal are impressed by the cleanliness of the property, but things take a turn for the worse when breakfast gets the thumbs down

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their critics face-to-face. Hosts of Trecarne House in Cornwall Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan raise issues with Magna Carta's Dominic Read on his mark for their hosting, while he has a bone to pick with them over criticism of his five-star floating B&B. Stephen and Joanna of A Room with a View in Brighton receive complaints about their breakfast, and tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo restores a pair of designer chairs, while Henry Cole breathes new life into a decrepit garden table, transforming it into a colourful and valuable new one

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly one-eyed South African porcupine Roxy is introduced to a new, younger male called Henning, in the hope that she will find companionship in her old age. Kifaru, the oldest eastern black rhino at Chester Zoo, meets younger female Malindi, with the expectation that they will produce offspring to increase the dwindling population, while the Caribbean flamingos are breeding again

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth visit Dick and Angel, who come up with some radical suggestions to change their business plans. Stephanie hopes to prepare for her first singles weekend by planting a romantic rose garden - if she can work the digger. Near the Pyrenees, Sophie and James need to raise funds for their ancient chateau fast, so they decide to put on a day of historic talks, dinner and a tour. Later, Dick and Angel struggle to rescue the trampoline that's been blown into their moat

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of a dual-purpose family and wheelchair-friendly home built by design engineer and former Paralympic swimmer Mark Butler. Mark was paralysed from the waist down following a motorcycle accident and the home he currently lives in with his family makes it virtually impossible for him to complete even the simplest of tasks around the house. He and his wife Penny want to build a cutting-edge family home, with a drive-in basement, passenger lift and accessible kitchen and bathroom. However, building such a unique property proves a huge challenge, and the strain on Mark and Penny becomes almost unbearable

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart represents an Olympic hopeful edged out of the US women's swim team due to a last-minute change to the eligibility window. Adrian is approached by the DNC with a compelling offer, while Diane and Julius unite to track down Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: New chancellor Sir Thomas More starts cracking down on religious extremists, but Anne Boleyn convinces the King he should at least consider Martin Luther's writings. Meanwhile, Cardinal Wolsey and Queen Catherine form an uneasy alliance to try to protect their positions, but it is rumours of a conspiracy involving them, the Pope and Charles V that finally force Henry's hand

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 71-year-old woman is rushed in after being knocked over by her husband's car, and her blood pressure has dropped critically low. Four-year-old Maddi is brought in with an injured neck after falling over on a train, and doctors worry that metal plates she already had in her neck may have shifted, causing damage to her spine. Plus, 68-year-old Bonnie arrives after hitting her head on her bedside table

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Claudia Winkleman take on Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Nick Helm to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max, Bloom and Reynolds recount a patient's past encounter that could throw them into a massive lawsuit. Iggy confronts a local middle school about its teaching policies, while Kapoor's superstitions get the better of him. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart represents an Olympic hopeful edged out of the US women's swim team due to a last-minute change to the eligibility window. Adrian is approached by the DNC with a compelling offer, while Diane and Julius unite to track down Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp creates some quick and easy 1960s-style pop art with a spray can to help transform Amber and Colin's living room

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Cliff and Angela from Nottingham fell in love with St Lucia when they were on a Caribbean cruise, and now, after discovering their pensions aren't performing as well as they'd like, they've decided to invest in a property there. Can Jasmine Harman find a place that will be a sound investment and also give them and their family an idyllic holiday home for years to come, all for their £120,000 budget?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog a huge range of possessions they unearth in Britain's barns and garages, to make money for their owners. In this episode, they celebrate Henry's birthday with some serious finds in Wensleydale in Yorkshire

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien assess sewing machines in Kent as they continue their journey across the UK to find unique and valuable items 'lost' in people's homes

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to track down the relatives of a man who became estranged from his family and died without a will, and an heir embarks on a journey of discovery in a bid to understand his late cousin's secret involvement in the Cold War. Plus, more details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to the Suffolk coast to meet Marisa and Andrew, who have just retired but are showing no signs of slowing down as they set out to build a bespoke home that will turn heads. However, when fate deals them a painful blow, this determined couple have to dig deep to get the job done

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynor's local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good night's sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his B&B. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but it's not long before they are dishing the dirt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm's owners tackle Gaynor's complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn an old, unloved filing cabinet into a valuable and desirable storage unit and convert a Bakelite voltmeter into a retro Bluetooth speaker

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Stomp, a 13-year-old male okapi, is being encouraged to mate with younger female K'Tusha. But on his first attempt, he snaps his horn and causes K'Tusha a minor injury. Cameras at Chester Zoo also follow African painted dogs as they compete to be pack leader, and Raja the Bornean river turtle meets two prospective mates

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth start to research their new business idea, but can they find the suppliers to help them? Stephanie's big event - her singles night - arrives, while Sophie and James put on a walking tour near their chateau

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of engineer Richard Bennett and his partner Felicia Böhm as they transform an underground water reservoir near the Humber Estuary into a family home. In 2013, the pair decided to embark on the ambitious build with a budget of only £150,000, but it proved a monumental challenge, and design and layout decisions stretched their abilities and the schedule to the max

programme.title: Deutschland 89
programme.desc: Martin is tortured by Valdez after being taken hosage, while Brigitte teams up with Schweppenstette and faces off with Lenora. Elsewhere, Tina and Ingrid uncover Tischbier's deepest secret. Drama, starring Jonas Nay. In German

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A road traffic accident caused 52-year-old Mark to suffer cardiac arrest, and as he battles for survival at St George's Hospital, his wife Jacquie reminisces about how their relationship blossomed from friendship into romance. A severe hip dislocation seems to be the diagnosis when 25-year-old Kayley arrives in A&E after being thrown from her horse, and 23-year-old receptionist Melissa books an appointment with nurse practitioner Craig to find out why she is having trouble hearing

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to collect Frances, an 80-year-old woman who fell three metres into her empty swimming pool while gardening, and has sustained a head wound that could have resulted in a life-altering bleed on the brain. Ruth, aged 86, arrives at St George's A&E with her heart beating three times faster than the normal rate. Doctors are concerned it may not be pumping enough blood around her body, and quickly prescribe fast-acting medication. Plus, students Jerry and Sakille turn up at the hospital after Jerry crashed her car on the way home from a party. While Jerry stayed sober to serve as the designated driver, she concludes that balancing a full-time job with her studies and a hectic social life might not be a sensible idea

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as team captain Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Aisling Bea. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A road traffic accident caused 52-year-old Mark to suffer cardiac arrest, and as he battles for survival at St George's Hospital, his wife Jacquie reminisces about how their relationship blossomed from friendship into romance. A severe hip dislocation seems to be the diagnosis when 25-year-old Kayley arrives in A&E after being thrown from her horse, and 23-year-old receptionist Melissa books an appointment with nurse practitioner Craig to find out why she is having trouble hearing

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to collect Frances, an 80-year-old woman who fell three metres into her empty swimming pool while gardening, and has sustained a head wound that could have resulted in a life-altering bleed on the brain. Ruth, aged 86, arrives at St George's A&E with her heart beating three times faster than the normal rate. Doctors are concerned it may not be pumping enough blood around her body, and quickly prescribe fast-acting medication. Plus, students Jerry and Sakille turn up at the hospital after Jerry crashed her car on the way home from a party. While Jerry stayed sober to serve as the designated driver, she concludes that balancing a full-time job with her studies and a hectic social life might not be a sensible idea

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Scotland to find out about a mysterious condition threatening salmon stocks, Kate Quilton heads Down Under to learn about kangaroo farming and Matt Tebbutt goes behind the scenes of the annual race to get new season asparagus onto the nation's supermarket shelves

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits southern Italy to find out where the bergamot flavour in Earl Grey tea comes from, before meeting a man who invented a method of making tea using an aerosol. Matt Tebbutt finds out why pork scratchings aren't made from British pigs and Kate Quilton is in a factory in California to uncover the secrets of jelly beans

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Fay Lewis has dreamed of moving to Spain for 20 years. Now, with her house in the UK sold and her bags packed, she's ready to take the plunge. Jasmine Harman shows Fay five properties around the Axarquia region of the Costa del Sol for her budget of 95,000 euros

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Tina Watkins has long struggled to get on the housing ladder in the UK. Now, she has decided to make the most of the incredibly good value property in Spain's Granada province and buy her first-ever home. With her best friend Jamie along to help, Jasmine Harman shows Tina just how far her £19,000 budget will stretch

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jon and Lynne Scopes from Stockport have thought about buying a property abroad for years and have viewed more than 40 apartments without success, but have at least decided their ideal area is Mijas on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jasmine Harman has the daunting task of finding them the perfect property for their £100,000 budget, but will anything meet their exacting standards?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house hunting in Essex and East London. Kirstie is searching for grandmother Janet, who is looking for a future-proof home where she will be able to live comfortably in the years to come. Meanwhile, Phil meets first-time buyers Adam and Becky, who are struggling to find a place to start a family

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search across two countries for two homes. Kirstie is with serial renters Mike and Poppy. Mike has lived in 18 homes, but Poppy wants to put down roots and they've chosen Cheshire as the right spot for their family. However, living in a high spec rental has given them great expectations for the home they'd love to own. Phil is in north Wales, helping nature-loving Pip, who's in search of a rural retreat, a chocolate-box character cottage with a garden big enough for chickens

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former property developer Ian hosts the first dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping his super-healthy menu will win him the £1,000 prize - although it is possible his guests may be more impressed with his 12-bedroom mansion in the country. Some naked antics, a pinata and stories of millions pounds won and lost make it a night to remember

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Then-Miss Cheltenham Sophie hosts the second dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping her evening of elegant food and pageantry will win her the £1,000 prize. The 24-year-old reveals what she does for a living to her guests, and one attendee makes a surprising confession of her own

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby-loving Carmel hosts the third dinner party in Gloucestershire, and she hopes to impress with a 'Six Nations' starter, a pancetta, king prawn and chorizo main, and a Key lime pie for dessert. An attempt to charm her guests backfires when she likens Ian to a psychotic killer, and the atmosphere takes a further knock after Chris gets on the wrong side of Sophie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: School teacher Polly, 40, hosts the fourth night of the competition in Gloucestershire, and hopes to impress her guests with a fine-dining experience. She begins with a seafood starter before serving up a meaty main course, and isn't unnerved by the fact she hasn't had any practise cooking either dish. However, her guests show off round the table and threaten to take the attention away from the night, but Ian is highly impressed with Polly's choice of entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host the final dinner party in Gloucestershire with home-made treats on the menu, including a tiger prawn starter, venison for the main and a dessert of chocolate and walnut ice-cream. Chat around the table reveals some differing opinions after the guests make a discovery in the host's living room, and the group reflect on their time together before the winner of the cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow B&B owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival B&B owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next day's breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: The ancient meadows get some new inhabitants when the family take delivery of some new beehives, while Matt prepares for lambing season and selects a ram with help from mum Janice. Billy the vet returns to check on the puppies before they leave for their forever homes, and the family reflects on the positive changes they have made since Janice's accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 84-year-old is rushed into hospital after losing control of his car and crashing into a road sign, and is in need of an emergency transfusion after his blood pressure drops dangerously low. Elsewhere, seven-year-old Reggie needs surgery to fix his broken arm, and 66-year-old Christine comes into A&E with worrying symptoms after the entire left side of her body collapses

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 61-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after a car mounted the pavement and ran her over and doctors quickly get her into the CT scanner to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Elsewhere, 91-year-old Sam arrives after suffering a suspected stroke, and four-month-old boy Teddy is rushed in after falling face first out of his car seat, hitting the pavement

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: Detroit Steel
programme.desc: Adam finds the perfect car for his client - a 1956 Chevrolet Bel Air station wagon and there is work to do as Mobsteel prepares for the shop's grand opening

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz unearth a musical treasure at an old general store, and a hoarder's house refuses to give up its secrets

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: The fortune-finding show continues in Bentwaters, with the contents of some old army bunkers exciting the bidders, while a vintage box captures Rupert's attention

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Two siblings are better than one when it comes to negotiating, which has Mike looking to bundle in order to save his bottom line

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Picking in New Mexico for the first time ever, the guys head straight to Roswell, but take a detour to a Wild West town with a million-dollar item for sale

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Sixty teams take on a very quick course down Windhaven Parkway in Fort Worth. Who will triumph in the Lone Star State?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough examination, while Richard Hammond charts the evolution of the Porsche 911 by pitting a modern Turbo S cabriolet against its oldest relative the Volkswagen Beetle - and the power of gravity. James May takes the Ariel Atom V8 for a spin on the test track, and comedian John Bishop receives tuition from the Stig before getting behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters square up to their Australian counterparts in an automotive version of the Ashes, with challenges including rallying, drifting and a double-decker car race. Jeremy Clarkson also test-drives the Ferrari 599 GTO, and Boris Becker gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef continues to explore his favourite locations in the Mediterranean, visiting a lagoon in Sardinia where he watches fishermen catching mullet using ancient Roman methods. He also boards a ferry heading for the city of Palermo in Sicily and discovers one of the most impressive pasta dishes of his career

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Albania to test three luxury vehicles - the Rolls-Royce Ghost, Bentley Mulsanne and Mercedes-Benz S65 AMG. Jeremy also drives three high-performance hatchbacks in the form of the limited-edition Ford Focus RS500, Prodrive-developed Cosworth Impreza STi CS400 and one-off Volvo C30 PPC. Jonathan Ross is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May shop for second-hand convertibles, uncovering the pitfalls to avoid when buying a used car, and Jeremy also gets the opportunity to test-drive the Pagani Zonda R. Actor Simon Pegg takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: The highs and lows of Prince Harry and Prince William's roles in the forces, featuring an interview with Harry in which he discusses a planned secret deployment in Afghanistan

programme.title: Private Lives of the Monarchs

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: The Royal Palace of Caserta near Naples, Italy, which was created by Luigi Vanvitelli and houses a silk factory that dates back to the 18th century and still functions to this day

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Kensington Palace in London, which began life as an early 17th-century Jacobean mansion before becoming a palace fit for the monarchy, and was the brainchild of Christopher Wren

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Germany, learning about an Edwardian fitness craze in Dresden before heading to Leipzig on a historic line built by British engineers in 1839. He discovers model-railway making on the grandest of scales in Hamburg and ends his journey in Kiel, where he reveals how British yachtsmen spied on the German navy in the early 20th century

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second visit in this series to Plas Newydd on the Isle of Anglesey. The team examine rarities including a cabinet with a secret function, a magazine rack made by celebrated designer Piero Fornasetti, a collection of historical items related to deafness, a Chinese silver punchbowl and a photo that has a connection to the Apollo 11 mission

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris journeys a thousand miles across Japan, starting out in Nagasaki, where the story of eastern railways began 150 years ago with the help of an enterprising Brit. In Fukuoka, he visits one of the Bullet Train's giant repair centres and in Hiroshima, he unravels what happened to its streetcar network after the world's first atomic bomb devastated the city. Along the way, Chris also meets a superstar cat and a singing conductor, while also checking into a popular trainspotter hotel and riding a steam-powered mountain railway

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A two-hour special featuring a deserted Caribbean city, an American ghost town still burning and an Arctic settlement facing a terrifying future

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: James Ellis is on the trail of Adolf Eichmann, the notorious Nazi bureaucrat who escaped justice for 15 years until his trial made the Holocaust a household name

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: An examination of how Leon Degrelle and an array of Finns, Estonians and fellow Belgians joined German volunteer brigades to hold off the Red Army on the Eastern front during the Second World War and enabled 56,000 men to escape

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second visit in this series to Plas Newydd on the Isle of Anglesey. The team examine rarities including a cabinet with a secret function, a magazine rack made by celebrated designer Piero Fornasetti, a collection of historical items related to deafness, a Chinese silver punchbowl and a photo that has a connection to the Apollo 11 mission

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores Scandinavia, braving one of the world's oldest rollercoasters in Copenhagen's Tivoli Gardens. He then crosses the Oresund Bridge linking Denmark to Sweden, retracing the journey of a train that carried a revolutionary Russian passenger on an epic voyage, before test-driving a vintage Volvo in Gothenburg. Crossing into Norway, Michael discovers how in 1913 the nation expressed its distinctively modern identity in plays, paintings and polar exploration

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: With no plans to guide them, an elite team of expert engineers re-create a unique two-seater version of the legendary Hawker Hurricane fighter

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the costal defence network built to defend Britain's beaches, including a tower in Essex which controlled mines laid across the estuary

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britain's military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The extension of the Panama Canal to accommodate the world's largest ships, which drew inspiration from the work of 17th-century engineer Hugues Cosnier

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How the world's biggest ship, designed to install oil platforms and lay pipelines, was inspired by a logging machine, a wire rope and 1940s shipbuilding techniques

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: Incredible feats of human endeavour, revealing what people have built, where they've built it, how and why. The first edition explores deep inside the rock on Gibraltar where there's over 55km complex of tunnels, under London, where 21st-century engineering innovation is paving the way for the ultimate super sewer and continues the legacy of Victorian pioneers and reveals the extraordinary subterranean space of one of Europe's largest and most breath-taking natural cave systems - The Postojna Cave in Slovenia

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A look at Sweden's passion for subterranean spaces and how this nation has taken extraordinary precautions to protect itself - by excavating an astounding 65,000 nuclear bunkers. In south-west England, deep in the Mendip Hills of Somerset, lies one of the most spectacular landscapes in the UK, but beneath this breath-taking landscape sits secret caverns and magical rock formations carved out by nature. Plus, how in Romania, nside a 1.2km-thick layer of salt, sits a world underground first

programme.title: Private Lives of the Monarchs
programme.desc: The life of Louis XIV, revealing the unpleasant truth behind the grandeur of the Palace of Versailles, and exploring the king's love life and unusual fashion sense

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Neuschwanstein Castle in the Alps of southern Germany. Created for eccentric king Ludwig the second in the 19th century, the castle is both a visual and engineering marvel that was an influence on Walt Disney. Containing a huge throne room and man-made cave, it took 17 years to build but was never fully completed after Ludwig was deposed, incarcerated and mysteriously died in 1868

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: A look at Edinburgh Castle - a symbol of Scottish might that has been besieged more than 20 times and has existed in many guises, including a military fortress, prison and palace

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores Scandinavia, braving one of the world's oldest rollercoasters in Copenhagen's Tivoli Gardens. He then crosses the Oresund Bridge linking Denmark to Sweden, retracing the journey of a train that carried a revolutionary Russian passenger on an epic voyage, before test-driving a vintage Volvo in Gothenburg. Crossing into Norway, Michael discovers how in 1913 the nation expressed its distinctively modern identity in plays, paintings and polar exploration

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. Objects brought in to be valued include a ship's anchor that was found in a garden pond, a medal awarded to sailor for helping to recover Captain Scott's ship from the Antarctic in 1901, some 17th-century flat pack furniture, and a flimsy booklet that proved to be a profitable investment

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris takes a railway trip across Canada, revealing how 2,500 miles of transcontinental railway was built against huge odds in just a few decades, helping to join together a vast wilderness of isolated communities and create the country that exists today. He begins his journey on one of the earliest sections of track to be built, on a train that is now a lifeline for hunters and fishermen. He then sees one of the biggest, most famous trains in the world - the Canadian, before heading for the Prairies, and on to the port of Vancouver. Along the way he also searches for Winnie the Pooh, crosses raging rivers and meets the descendant of a pioneering Yorkshire family who arrived here more than 100 years ago

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A juggernaut of Soviet engineering which was crucial to the international space race. Deserted Scottish coastal defences and a complex that's crucial to American history

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The mysterious Cachtice Castle home to Elizabeth Bathory, said to have killed over 600 girls, and a mysterious structure on the 38th parallel

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: A profile of Albert Speer, the architectural mastermind of the Third Reich who escaped the noose. James Ellis believes there's a monstrous truth behind Speer's lies

programme.title: Churchill's Secret Son
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Tipperary-born businessman and Conservative cabinet minister Brendan Bracken, who became Winston Churchill's right-hand man during the Second World War by opposing efforts to appease Adolf Hitler. The film considers the possibility of a biological connection between the two men

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. Objects brought in to be valued include a ship's anchor that was found in a garden pond, a medal awarded to sailor for helping to recover Captain Scott's ship from the Antarctic in 1901, some 17th-century flat pack furniture, and a flimsy booklet that proved to be a profitable investment

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munich's pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The series examining some of the UK's landmarks continues, with a look at the history of subterranean London, uncovering Roman remains, plague pits and secret bunkers under the capital's streets

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
